READBUFFER_MIN  1024\n\n/* The default upload buffer size, should not be smaller than\n   CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE, as it needs to hold a full buffer as could be sent in\n   a write callback.\n\n   The size was 16KB for many years but was bumped to 64KB because it makes\n   libcurl able to do significantly faster uploads in some circumstances. Even\n   larger buffers can help further, but this is deemed a fair memory/speed\n   compromise. */\n#define UPLOADBUFFER_DEFAULT 65536\n#define UPLOADBUFFER_MAX     (2 * 1024 * 1024)\n#define UPLOADBUFFER_MIN     CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE\n\n#define CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER 0xc0dedbadU\n#ifdef DEBUGBUILD\n/* On a debug build, we want to fail hard on easy handles that\n * are not NULL, but no longer have the MAGIC touch. This gives\n * us early warning on things only discovered by valgrind otherwise. */\n#define GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(x) \\\n  (((x) && ((x)->magic == CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER)) ? TRUE : \\\n   (DEBUGASSERT(!(x)), FALSE))\n#else\n#define GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(x) \\\n  ((x) && ((x)->magic == CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER))\n#endif\n\nstruct ssl_primary_config {\n  char *CApath;          /* certificate directory (does not work on Windows) */\n  char *CAfile;          /* certificate to verify peer against */\n  char *issuercert;      /* optional issuer certificate filename */\n  char *clientcert;\n  char *cipher_list;     /* list of ciphers to use */\n  char *cipher_list13;   /* list of TLS 1.3 cipher suites to use */\n  char *signature_algorithms; /* list of signature algorithms to use */\n  char *pinned_key;\n  char *CRLfile;         /* CRL to check certificate revocation */\n  struct curl_blob *cert_blob;\n  struct curl_blob *ca_info_blob;\n  struct curl_blob *issuercert_blob;\n#ifdef USE_TLS_SRP\n  char *username; /* TLS username (for, e.g., SRP) */\n  char *password; /* TLS password (for, e.g., SRP) */\n#endif\n  char *curves;          /* list of curves to use */\n  uint32_t version_max; /* max supported version the client wants to use */\n  uint8_t ssl_options;  /* the CURLOPT_SSL_OPTIONS bitmask */\n  uint8_t version;    /* what version the client wants to use */\n  BIT(verifypeer);       /* set TRUE if this is desired */\n  BIT(verifyhost);       /* set TRUE if CN/SAN must match hostname */\n  BIT(verifystatus);     /* set TRUE if certificate status must be checked */\n  BIT(cache_session);    /* cache session or not */\n};\n\nstruct ssl_config_data {\n  struct ssl_primary_config primary;\n  long certverifyresult; /* result from the certificate verification */\n  curl_ssl_ctx_callback fsslctx; /* function to initialize ssl ctx */\n  void *fsslctxp;        /* parameter for call back */\n  char *cert_type; /* format for certificate (default: PEM) */\n  char *key; /* private key filename */\n  struct curl_blob *key_blob;\n  char *key_type; /* format for private key (default: PEM) */\n  char *key_passwd; /* plain text private key password */\n  BIT(certinfo);     /* gather lots of certificate info */\n  BIT(earlydata);    /* use TLS 1.3 early data */\n  BIT(enable_beast); /* allow this flaw for interoperability's sake */\n  BIT(no_revoke);    /* disable SSL certificate revocation checks */\n  BIT(no_partialchain); /* do not accept partial certificate chains */\n  BIT(revoke_best_effort); /* ignore SSL revocation offline/missing revocation\n                              list errors */\n  BIT(native_ca_store); /* use the native CA store of operating system */\n  BIT(auto_client_cert);   /* automatically locate and use a client\n                              certificate for authentication (Schannel) */\n  BIT(custom_cafile); /* application has set custom CA file */\n  BIT(custom_capath); /* application has set custom CA path */\n  BIT(custom_cablob); /* application has set custom CA blob */\n};\n\nstruct ssl_general_config {\n  int ca_cache_timeout;  /* Certificate store cache timeout (seconds) */\n};\n\n#ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\n#include \"curl_sspi.h\"\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DIGEST_AUTH\n/* Struct used for Digest challenge-response authentication */\nstruct digestdata {\n#ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\n  BYTE *input_token;\n  size_t input_token_len;\n  CtxtHandle *http_context;\n  /* copy of user/passwd used to make the identity for http_context.\n     either may be NULL. */\n  char *user;\n  char *passwd;\n#else\n  char *nonce;\n  char *cnonce;\n  char *realm;\n  char *opaque;\n  char *qop;\n  char *algorithm;\n  int nc; /* nonce count */\n  uint8_t algo;\n  BIT(stale); /* set true for re-negotiation */\n  BIT(userhash);\n#endif\n};\n#endif\n\ntypedef enum {\n  NTLMSTATE_NONE,\n  NTLMSTATE_TYPE1,\n  NTLMSTATE_TYPE2,\n  NTLMSTATE_TYPE3,\n  NTLMSTATE_LAST\n} curlntlm;\n\ntypedef enum {\n  GSS_AUTHNONE,\n  GSS_AUTHRECV,\n  GSS_AUTHSENT,\n  GSS_AUTHDONE,\n  GSS_AUTHSUCC\n} curlnegotiate;\n\n#ifdef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n#define CONN_IS_PROXIED(x) 0\n#else\n#define CONN_IS_PROXIED(x) (x)->bits.proxy\n#endif\n\n/*\n * Boolean values that concerns this connection.\n */\nstruct ConnectBits {\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  BIT(httpproxy);  /* if set, this transfer is done through an HTTP proxy */\n  BIT(socksproxy); /* if set, this transfer is done through a socks proxy */\n  BIT(proxy_user_passwd); /* user+password for the proxy? */\n  BIT(tunnel_proxy);  /* if CONNECT is used to \"tunnel\" through the proxy.\n                         This is implicit when SSL-protocols are used through\n                         proxies, but can also be enabled explicitly by\n                         apps */\n  BIT(proxy); /* if set, this transfer is done through a proxy - any type */\n#endif\n  /* always modify bits.close with the connclose() and connkeep() macros! */\n  BIT(close); /* if set, we close the connection after this request */\n  BIT(reuse); /* if set, this is a reused connection */\n  BIT(altused); /* this is an alt-svc \"redirect\" */\n  BIT(conn_to_host); /* if set, this connection has a \"connect to host\"\n                        that overrides the host in the URL */\n  BIT(conn_to_port); /* if set, this connection has a \"connect to port\"\n                        that overrides the port in the URL (remote port) */\n  BIT(ipv6_ip); /* we communicate with a remote site specified with pure IPv6\n                   IP address */\n  BIT(ipv6);    /* we communicate with a site using an IPv6 address */\n  BIT(do_more); /* this is set TRUE if the ->curl_do_more() function is\n                   supposed to be called, after ->curl_do() */\n  BIT(protoconnstart);/* the protocol layer has STARTED its operation after\n                         the TCP layer connect */\n  BIT(retry);         /* this connection is about to get closed and then\n                         re-attempted at another connection. */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  BIT(ftp_use_epsv);  /* As set with CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPSV, but if we find out\n                         EPSV does not work we disable it for the forthcoming\n                         requests */\n  BIT(ftp_use_eprt);  /* As set with CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPRT, but if we find out\n                         EPRT does not work we disable it for the forthcoming\n                         requests */\n  BIT(ftp_use_data_ssl); /* Enabled SSL for the data connection */\n  BIT(ftp_use_control_ssl); /* Enabled SSL for the control connection */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_NETRC\n  BIT(netrc);         /* name+password provided by netrc */\n#endif\n  BIT(bound); /* set true if bind() has already been done on this socket/\n                 connection */\n  BIT(upgrade_in_progress); /* protocol upgrade is in progress */\n  BIT(multiplex); /* connection is multiplexed */\n  BIT(tcp_fastopen); /* use TCP Fast Open */\n  BIT(tls_enable_alpn); /* TLS ALPN extension? */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  BIT(doh);\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  BIT(abstract_unix_socket);\n#endif\n  BIT(sock_accepted); /* TRUE if the SECONDARYSOCKET was created with\n                         accept() */\n  BIT(parallel_connect); /* set TRUE when a parallel connect attempt has\n                            started (happy eyeballs) */\n  BIT(aborted); /* connection was aborted, e.g. in unclean state */\n  BIT(no_reuse); /* connection should not be reused */\n  BIT(shutdown_handler); /* connection shutdown: handler shut down */\n  BIT(shutdown_filters); /* connection shutdown: filters shut down */\n  BIT(in_cpool);     /* connection is kept in a connection pool */\n};\n\nstruct hostname {\n  char *rawalloc; /* allocated \"raw\" version of the name */\n  char *encalloc; /* allocated IDN-encoded version of the name */\n  char *name;     /* name to use internally, might be encoded, might be raw */\n  const char *dispname; /* name to display, as 'name' might be encoded */\n};\n\n/*\n * Flags on the keepon member of the Curl_transfer_keeper\n */\n\n#define KEEP_NONE       0\n#define KEEP_RECV       (1 << 0) /* there is or may be data to read */\n#define KEEP_SEND       (1 << 1) /* there is or may be data to write */\n\n/* transfer wants to send */\n#define CURL_WANT_SEND(data) ((data)->req.keepon & KEEP_SEND)\n/* transfer wants to receive */\n#define CURL_WANT_RECV(data) ((data)->req.keepon & KEEP_RECV)\n\n#define FIRSTSOCKET     0\n#define SECONDARYSOCKET 1\n\n/*\n * Specific protocol handler.\n */\n\nstruct Curl_protocol {\n  /* Complement to setup_connection_internals(). This is done before the\n     transfer \"owns\" the connection. */\n  CURLcode (*setup_connection)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               struct connectdata *conn);\n\n  /* These two functions MUST be set to be protocol dependent */\n  CURLcode (*do_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n  CURLcode (*done)(struct Curl_easy *, CURLcode, bool);\n\n  /* If the curl_do() function is better made in two halves, this\n   * curl_do_more() function will be called afterwards, if set. For example\n   * for doing the FTP stuff after the PASV/PORT command.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*do_more)(struct Curl_easy *, int *);\n\n  /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\n   * after the connect() and everything is done, as a step in the connection.\n   * The 'done' pointer points to a bool that should be set to TRUE if the\n   * function completes before return. If it does not complete, the caller\n   * should call the ->connecting() function until it is.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*connect_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n\n  /* See above. */\n  CURLcode (*connecting)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n  CURLcode (*doing)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the PROTOCONNECT phase, and it\n     should then return a proper fd set */\n  CURLcode (*proto_pollset)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                            struct easy_pollset *ps);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DOING phase, and it should\n     then return a proper fd set */\n  CURLcode (*doing_pollset)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                            struct easy_pollset *ps);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_MORE phase, and it should\n     then return a proper fd set */\n  CURLcode (*domore_pollset)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                            struct easy_pollset *ps);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_DONE, PERFORM and\n     WAITPERFORM phases, and it should then return a proper fd set. Not setting\n     this will make libcurl use the generic default one. */\n  CURLcode (*perform_pollset)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                              struct easy_pollset *ps);\n\n  /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\n   * by the curl_disconnect(), as a step in the disconnection. If the handler\n   * is called because the connection has been considered dead,\n   * dead_connection is set to TRUE. The connection is (again) associated with\n   * the transfer here.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*disconnect)(struct Curl_easy *, struct connectdata *,\n                         bool dead_connection);\n\n  /* If used, this function gets called from transfer.c to\n     allow the protocol to do extra handling in writing response to\n     the client. */\n  CURLcode (*write_resp)(struct Curl_easy *data, const char *buf, size_t blen,\n                         bool is_eos);\n\n  /* If used, this function gets called from transfer.c to\n     allow the protocol to do extra handling in writing a single response\n     header line to the client. */\n  CURLcode (*write_resp_hd)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                            const char *hd, size_t hdlen, bool is_eos);\n\n  /* This function can perform various checks on the connection. See\n     CONNCHECK_* for more information about the checks that can be performed,\n     and CONNRESULT_* for the results that can be returned. */\n  uint32_t (*connection_check)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               struct connectdata *conn,\n                               uint32_t checks_to_perform);\n\n  /* attach() attaches this transfer to this connection */\n  void (*attach)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn);\n\n  /* return CURLE_OK if a redirect to `newurl` should be followed,\n     CURLE_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS otherwise. May alter `data` to change\n     the way the follow request is performed. */\n  CURLcode (*follow)(struct Curl_easy *data, const char *newurl,\n                     followtype type);\n};\n\nstruct Curl_scheme {\n  const char *name;       /* URL scheme name in lowercase */\n  const struct Curl_protocol *run; /* implementation */\n  curl_prot_t protocol;   /* See CURLPROTO_* - this needs to be the single\n                             specific protocol bit */\n  curl_prot_t family;     /* single bit for protocol family; basically the\n                             non-TLS name of the protocol this is */\n  uint32_t flags;         /* Extra particular characteristics, see PROTOPT_* */\n  uint16_t defport;       /* Default port. */\n};\n\n#define PROTOPT_NONE 0             /* nothing extra */\n#define PROTOPT_SSL (1 << 0)       /* uses SSL */\n#define PROTOPT_DUAL (1 << 1)      /* this protocol uses two connections */\n#define PROTOPT_CLOSEACTION (1 << 2) /* need action before socket close */\n/* some protocols will have to call the underlying functions without regard to\n   what exact state the socket signals. IE even if the socket says \"readable\",\n   the send function might need to be called while uploading, or vice versa.\n*/\n#define PROTOPT_DIRLOCK (1 << 3)\n#define PROTOPT_NONETWORK (1 << 4) /* protocol does not use the network! */\n#define PROTOPT_NEEDSPWD (1 << 5)  /* needs a password, and if none is set it\n                                      gets a default */\n#define PROTOPT_NOURLQUERY (1 << 6)  /* protocol cannot handle\n                                        URL query strings (?foo=bar) ! */\n#define PROTOPT_CREDSPERREQUEST (1 << 7) /* requires login credentials per\n                                            request instead of per\n                                            connection */\n#define PROTOPT_ALPN (1 << 8) /* set ALPN for this */\n/* (1 << 9) was PROTOPT_STREAM, now free */\n#define PROTOPT_URLOPTIONS (1 << 10) /* allow options part in the userinfo\n                                        field of the URL */\n#define PROTOPT_PROXY_AS_HTTP (1 << 11) /* allow this non-HTTP scheme over a\n                                           HTTP proxy as HTTP proxies may know\n                                           this protocol and act as\n                                           a gateway */\n#define PROTOPT_WILDCARD (1 << 12)  /* protocol supports wildcard matching */\n#define PROTOPT_USERPWDCTRL (1 << 13) /* Allow \"control bytes\" (< 32 ASCII) in\n                                         username and password */\n#define PROTOPT_NOTCPPROXY (1 << 14)  /* this protocol cannot proxy over TCP */\n#define PROTOPT_SSL_REUSE (1 << 15)   /* this protocol may reuse an existing\n                                         SSL connection in the same family\n                                         without having PROTOPT_SSL. */\n#define PROTOPT_CONN_REUSE (1 << 16)  /* this protocol can reuse connections */\n\n#define CONNCHECK_NONE 0                 /* No checks */\n#define CONNCHECK_ISDEAD (1 << 0)        /* Check if the connection is dead. */\n#define CONNCHECK_KEEPALIVE (1 << 1)     /* Perform any keepalive function. */\n\n#define CONNRESULT_NONE 0                /* No extra information. */\n#define CONNRESULT_DEAD (1 << 0)         /* The connection is dead. */\n\n#define TRNSPRT_NONE 0\n#define TRNSPRT_TCP  3\n#define TRNSPRT_UDP  4\n#define TRNSPRT_QUIC 5\n#define TRNSPRT_UNIX 6\n\nstruct ip_quadruple {\n  char remote_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  char local_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  uint16_t remote_port;\n  uint16_t local_port;\n  uint8_t transport;\n};\n\n#define CUR_IP_QUAD_HAS_PORTS(x)      \\\n  (((x)->transport == TRNSPRT_TCP) || \\\n   ((x)->transport == TRNSPRT_UDP) || \\\n   ((x)->transport == TRNSPRT_QUIC))\n\nstruct proxy_info {\n  struct hostname host;\n  uint16_t port;\n  uint8_t proxytype; /* what kind of proxy that is in use */\n  char *user;    /* proxy username string, allocated */\n  char *passwd;  /* proxy password string, allocated */\n};\n\n/*\n * The connectdata struct contains all fields and variables that should be\n * unique for an entire connection.\n */\nstruct connectdata {\n  struct Curl_llist_node cpool_node; /* conncache lists */\n  struct Curl_llist_node cshutdn_node; /* cshutdn list */\n\n  curl_closesocket_callback fclosesocket; /* function closing the socket(s) */\n  void *closesocket_client;\n\n  /* This is used by the connection pool logic. If this returns TRUE, this\n     handle is still used by one or more easy handles and can only used by any\n     other easy handle without careful consideration (== only for\n     multiplexing) and it cannot be used by another multi handle! */\n#define CONN_INUSE(c) (!!(c)->attached_xfers)\n\n  /**** Fields set when inited and not modified again */\n  curl_off_t connection_id; /* Contains a unique number to make it easier to\n                               track the connections in the log output */\n  char *destination; /* string carrying normalized hostname+port+scope */\n\n  /* `meta_hash` is a general key-value store for implementations\n   * with the lifetime of the connection.\n   * Elements need to be added with their own destructor to be invoked when\n   * the connection is cleaned up (see Curl_hash_add2()).*/\n  struct Curl_hash meta_hash;\n\n  struct hostname host;\n  char *hostname_resolve; /* hostname to resolve to address, allocated */\n  char *secondaryhostname; /* secondary socket hostname (ftp) */\n  struct hostname conn_to_host; /* the host to connect to. valid only if\n                                   bits.conn_to_host is set */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  struct proxy_info socks_proxy;\n  struct proxy_info http_proxy;\n#endif\n  char *user;    /* username string, allocated */\n  char *passwd;  /* password string, allocated */\n  char *options; /* options string, allocated */\n  char *sasl_authzid;     /* authorization identity string, allocated */\n  char *oauth_bearer; /* OAUTH2 bearer, allocated */\n  struct curltime created; /* creation time */\n  struct curltime lastused; /* when returned to the connection poolas idle */\n\n  /* A connection can have one or two sockets and connection filters.\n   * The protocol using the 2nd one is FTP for CONTROL+DATA sockets */\n  curl_socket_t sock[2];\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cfilter[2]; /* connection filters */\n  Curl_recv *recv[2];\n  Curl_send *send[2];\n  int recv_idx;  /* on which socket index to receive, default 0 */\n  int send_idx;  /* on which socket index to send, default 0 */\n\n#define CONN_SOCK_IDX_VALID(i)    (((i) >= 0) && ((i) < 2))\n\n  struct {\n    struct curltime start[2]; /* when filter shutdown started */\n    timediff_t timeout_ms; /* 0 means no timeout */\n  } shutdown;\n\n  struct ssl_primary_config ssl_config;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  struct ssl_primary_config proxy_ssl_config;\n#endif\n  struct ConnectBits bits;    /* various state-flags for this connection */\n\n  const struct Curl_scheme *scheme; /* Connection's protocol handler */\n  const struct Curl_scheme *given;   /* The protocol first given */\n\n  /* Protocols can use a custom keepalive mechanism to keep connections alive.\n     This allows those protocols to track the last time the keepalive mechanism\n     was used on this connection. */\n  struct curltime keepalive;\n\n  /* A connection cache from a SHARE might be used in several multi handles.\n   * We MUST not reuse connections that are running in another multi,\n   * for concurrency reasons. That multi might run in another thread.\n   * `attached_multi` is set by the first transfer attached and cleared\n   * when the last one is detached.\n   * NEVER call anything on this multi, just check for equality. */\n  struct Curl_multi *attached_multi;\n\n  /*************** Request - specific items ************/\n#if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI) && defined(SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS)\n  CtxtHandle *sslContext;  /* mingw-w64 v9+. MS SDK 7.0A+. */\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_NTLM\n  curlntlm http_ntlm_state;\n  curlntlm proxy_ntlm_state;\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_SPNEGO\n  curlnegotiate http_negotiate_state;\n  curlnegotiate proxy_negotiate_state;\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  char *unix_domain_socket;\n#endif\n\n  /* When this connection is created, store the conditions for the local end\n     bind. This is stored before the actual bind and before any connection is\n     made and will serve the purpose of being used for comparison reasons so\n     that subsequent bound-requested connections are not accidentally reusing\n     wrong connections. */\n  char *localdev;\n  uint16_t localportrange;\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  int socks5_gssapi_enctype;\n#endif\n  /* The field below gets set in connect.c:connecthost() */\n  int remote_port; /* the remote port, not the proxy port! */\n  int conn_to_port; /* the remote port to connect to. valid only if\n                       bits.conn_to_port is set */\n\n  uint32_t attached_xfers; /* # of attached easy handles */\n\n#ifdef USE_IPV6\n  uint32_t scope_id;  /* Scope id for IPv6 */\n#endif\n  uint16_t localport;\n  uint16_t secondary_port; /* secondary socket remote port to connect to\n                                    (ftp) */\n  uint8_t transport_wanted; /* one of the TRNSPRT_* defines. Not\n   necessarily the transport the connection ends using due to Alt-Svc\n   and happy eyeballing. Use `Curl_conn_get_transport() for actual value\n   once the connection is set up. */\n  uint8_t ip_version; /* copied from the Curl_easy at creation time */\n  /* HTTP version last responded with by the server or negotiated via ALPN.\n   * 0 at start, then one of 09, 10, 11, etc. */\n  uint8_t httpversion_seen;\n  uint8_t gssapi_delegation; /* inherited from set.gssapi_delegation */\n  BIT(connect_only);\n};\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n#define CURL_CONN_HOST_DISPNAME(c) \\\n  ((c)->bits.socksproxy ? (c)->socks_proxy.host.dispname : \\\n    (c)->bits.httpproxy ? (c)->http_proxy.host.dispname : \\\n      (c)->bits.conn_to_host ? (c)->conn_to_host.dispname : \\\n        (c)->host.dispname)\n#else\n#define CURL_CONN_HOST_DISPNAME(c) \\\n  (c)->bits.conn_to_host ? (c)->conn_to_host.dispname : \\\n    (c)->host.dispname\n#endif\n\n/* The end of connectdata. */\n\n/*\n * Struct to keep statistical and informational data.\n * All variables in this struct must be initialized/reset in Curl_initinfo().\n */\nstruct PureInfo {\n  int httpcode;  /* Recent HTTP, FTP, RTSP or SMTP response code */\n  int httpproxycode; /* response code from proxy when received separate */\n  int httpversion; /* the http version number X.Y = X*10+Y */\n  time_t filetime; /* If requested, this is might get set. Set to -1 if the\n                      time was unretrievable. */\n  curl_off_t request_size; /* the amount of bytes sent in the request(s) */\n  curl_off_t numconnects; /* how many new connections libcurl created */\n  uint32_t proxyauthavail; /* what proxy auth types were announced */\n  uint32_t httpauthavail;  /* what host auth types were announced */\n  uint32_t proxyauthpicked; /* selected proxy auth type */\n  uint32_t httpauthpicked;  /* selected host auth type */\n  char *contenttype; /* the content type of the object */\n  char *wouldredirect; /* URL this would have been redirected to if asked to */\n  curl_off_t retry_after; /* info from Retry-After: header */\n  uint32_t header_size;  /* size of read header(s) in bytes */\n\n  /* PureInfo primary ip_quadruple is copied over from the connectdata\n     struct in order to allow curl_easy_getinfo() to return this information\n     even when the session handle is no longer associated with a connection,\n     and also allow curl_easy_reset() to clear this information from the\n     session handle without disturbing information which is still alive, and\n     that might be reused, in the connection pool. */\n  struct ip_quadruple primary;\n  int conn_remote_port;  /* this is the \"remote port\", which is the port\n                            number of the used URL, independent of proxy or\n                            not */\n  const char *conn_scheme;\n  uint32_t conn_protocol;\n  struct curl_certinfo certs; /* info about the certs. Asked for with\n                                 CURLOPT_CERTINFO / CURLINFO_CERTINFO */\n  CURLproxycode pxcode;\n  BIT(timecond);  /* set to TRUE if the time condition did not match, which\n                     thus made the document NOT get fetched */\n  BIT(used_proxy); /* the transfer used a proxy */\n};\n\nstruct pgrs_dir {\n  curl_off_t total_size; /* total expected bytes */\n  curl_off_t cur_size; /* transferred bytes so far */\n  curl_off_t speed; /* bytes per second transferred */\n  struct Curl_rlimit rlimit; /* speed limiting / pausing */\n};\n\nstruct Progress {\n  struct curltime now; /* current time of processing */\n  time_t lastshow; /* time() of the last displayed progress meter or NULL to\n                      force redraw at next call */\n  struct pgrs_dir ul;\n  struct pgrs_dir dl;\n\n  curl_off_t current_speed; /* uses the currently fastest transfer */\n  curl_off_t earlydata_sent;\n\n  timediff_t timespent;\n\n  timediff_t t_postqueue;\n  timediff_t t_nslookup;\n  timediff_t t_connect;\n  timediff_t t_appconnect;\n  timediff_t t_pretransfer;\n  timediff_t t_posttransfer;\n  timediff_t t_starttransfer;\n  timediff_t t_redirect;\n\n  struct curltime start;\n  struct curltime t_startsingle;\n  struct curltime t_startop;\n  struct curltime t_startqueue;\n  struct curltime t_acceptdata;\n\n#define CURL_SPEED_RECORDS (5 + 1) /* 6 entries for 5 seconds */\n\n  curl_off_t speed_amount[CURL_SPEED_RECORDS];\n  struct curltime speed_time[CURL_SPEED_RECORDS];\n  uint8_t speeder_c;\n  BIT(hide);\n  BIT(ul_size_known);\n  BIT(dl_size_known);\n  BIT(headers_out); /* when the headers have been written */\n  BIT(callback);  /* set when progress callback is used */\n  BIT(is_t_startransfer_set);\n};\n\ntypedef enum {\n  RTSPREQ_NONE, /* first in list */\n  RTSPREQ_OPTIONS,\n  RTSPREQ_DESCRIBE,\n  RTSPREQ_ANNOUNCE,\n  RTSPREQ_SETUP,\n  RTSPREQ_PLAY,\n  RTSPREQ_PAUSE,\n  RTSPREQ_TEARDOWN,\n  RTSPREQ_GET_PARAMETER,\n  RTSPREQ_SET_PARAMETER,\n  RTSPREQ_RECORD,\n  RTSPREQ_RECEIVE,\n  RTSPREQ_LAST /* last in list */\n} Curl_RtspReq;\n\nstruct auth {\n  uint32_t want;  /* Bitmask set to the authentication methods wanted by app\n                     (with CURLOPT_HTTPAUTH or CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH). */\n  uint32_t picked;\n  uint32_t avail; /* Bitmask for what the server reports to support for this\n                     resource */\n  BIT(done);  /* TRUE when the auth phase is done and ready to do the\n                 actual request */\n  BIT(multipass); /* TRUE if this is not yet authenticated but within the\n                     auth multipass negotiation */\n  BIT(iestyle); /* TRUE if digest should be done IE-style or FALSE if it\n                   should be RFC compliant */\n};\n\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\nstruct Curl_data_prio_node {\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *next;\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n};\n#endif\n\n/**\n * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others\n * on the same connection.\n */\nstruct Curl_data_priority {\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */\n  struct Curl_easy *parent;\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;\n#endif\n  int weight;\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  BIT(exclusive);\n#endif\n};\n\n/* Timers */\ntypedef enum {\n  EXPIRE_100_TIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_ASYNC_NAME,\n  EXPIRE_CONNECTTIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME, /* family1 */\n  EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME2, /* family2 */\n  EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS_DNS, /* See asyn-ares.c */\n  EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS,\n  EXPIRE_MULTI_PENDING,\n  EXPIRE_SPEEDCHECK,\n  EXPIRE_TIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_TOOFAST,\n  EXPIRE_QUIC,\n  EXPIRE_FTP_ACCEPT,\n  EXPIRE_ALPN_EYEBALLS,\n  EXPIRE_SHUTDOWN,\n  EXPIRE_LAST /* not an actual timer, used as a marker only */\n} expire_id;\n\ntypedef enum {\n  TRAILERS_NONE,\n  TRAILERS_INITIALIZED,\n  TRAILERS_SENDING,\n  TRAILERS_DONE\n} trailers_state;\n\n/*\n * One instance for each timeout an easy handle can set.\n */\nstruct time_node {\n  struct Curl_llist_node list;\n  struct curltime time;\n  expire_id eid;\n};\n\n/* individual pieces of the URL */\nstruct urlpieces {\n  char *scheme;\n  char *hostname;\n  char *port;\n  char *user;\n  char *password;\n  char *options;\n  char *path;\n  char *query;\n};\n\n#define CREDS_NONE   0\n#define CREDS_URL    1 /* from URL */\n#define CREDS_OPTION 2 /* set with a CURLOPT_ */\n#define CREDS_NETRC  3 /* found in netrc */\n\nstruct UrlState {\n  /* buffers to store authentication data in, as parsed from input options */\n  struct curltime keeps_speed; /* for the progress meter really */\n\n  curl_off_t lastconnect_id; /* The last connection, -1 if undefined */\n  curl_off_t recent_conn_id; /* The most recent connection used, might no\n                              * longer exist */\n  struct dynbuf headerb; /* buffer to store headers in */\n  struct curl_slist *hstslist; /* list of HSTS files set by\n                                  curl_easy_setopt(HSTS) calls */\n  curl_off_t current_speed;  /* the ProgressShow() function sets this,\n                                bytes / second */\n\n  /* hostname, port number and protocol of the first (not followed) request.\n     if set, this should be the hostname that we will sent authorization to,\n     no else. Used to make Location: following not keep sending user+password.\n     This is strdup()ed data. */\n  char *first_host;\n  int first_remote_port;\n  curl_prot_t first_remote_protocol;\n  int os_errno;  /* filled in with errno whenever an error occurs */\n  int requests; /* request counter: redirects + authentication retakes */\n#ifdef HAVE_SIGNAL\n  /* storage for the previous bag^H^H^HSIGPIPE signal handler :-) */\n  void (*prev_signal)(int sig);\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DIGEST_AUTH\n  struct digestdata digest;      /* state data for host Digest auth */\n  struct digestdata proxydigest; /* state data for proxy Digest auth */\n#endif\n  struct auth authhost;  /* auth details for host */\n  struct auth authproxy; /* auth details for proxy */\n\n  struct Curl_dns_entry *dns[2]; /* DNS to connect FIRST/SECONDARY */\n#ifdef USE_CURL_ASYNC\n  struct Curl_async async;  /* asynchronous name resolver data */\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL\n  /* void instead of ENGINE to avoid bleeding OpenSSL into this header */\n  void *engine;\n  /* void instead of OSSL_PROVIDER */\n  void *provider;\n  void *baseprov;\n  void *libctx;\n  char *propq; /* for a provider */\n\n  BIT(provider_loaded);\n#endif /* USE_OPENSSL */\n  struct curltime expiretime; /* set this with Curl_expire() only */\n  struct Curl_tree timenode; /* for the splay stuff */\n  struct Curl_llist timeoutlist; /* list of pending timeouts */\n  struct time_node expires[EXPIRE_LAST]; /* nodes for each expire type */\n\n  /* a place to store the most recently set (S)FTP entrypath */\n  char *most_recent_ftp_entrypath;\n  char *range; /* range, if used. /* the session's next client CSeq */\n  uint32_t rtsp_next_server_CSeq; /* the session's next server CSeq */\n  uint32_t rtsp_CSeq_recv; /* most recent CSeq received */\n  uint8_t rtp_channel_mask[32]; /* for the correctness checking of the\n                                         interleaved data */\n#endif\n\n  curl_off_t infilesize; /* size of file to upload, -1 means unknown.\n                            Copied from set.filesize at start of operation */\n#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  struct Curl_data_priority priority; /* shallow copy of data->set */\n#endif\n\n  curl_read_callback fread_func; /* read callback/function */\n  void *in;                      /* CURLOPT_READDATA */\n  CURLU *uh; /* URL handle for the current parsed URL */\n  struct urlpieces up;\n  struct bufref url;        /* work URL, initially copied from UserDefined */\n  struct bufref referer;    /* referer string */\n  struct curl_slist *resolve; /* set to point to the set.resolve list when\n                                 this should be dealt with in pretransfer */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  curl_mimepart *mimepost;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FORM_API\n  curl_mimepart *formp; /* storage for old API form-posting, allocated on\n                           demand */\n#endif\n  size_t trailers_bytes_sent;\n  struct dynbuf trailers_buf; /* a buffer containing the compiled trailing\n                                 headers */\n  struct Curl_llist httphdrs; /* where is the server credentials originating\n                                from, see the CREDS_* defines above */\n\n  /* when curl_easy_perform() is called, the multi handle is \"owned\" by\n     the easy handle so curl_easy_cleanup() on such an easy handle will\n     also close the multi handle! */\n  BIT(multi_owned_by_easy);\n\n  BIT(this_is_a_follow); /* this is a followed Location: request */\n  BIT(refused_stream); /* this was refused, try again */\n  BIT(errorbuf); /* Set to TRUE if the error buffer is already filled in.\n                    This must be set to FALSE every time _easy_perform() is\n                    called. */\n  BIT(allow_port); /* Is set.use_port allowed to take effect or not. This\n                      is always set TRUE when curl_easy_perform() is called. */\n  BIT(authproblem); /* TRUE if there is some problem authenticating */\n  /* set after initial USER failure, to prevent an authentication loop */\n  BIT(wildcardmatch); /* enable wildcard matching */\n  BIT(disableexpect);    /* TRUE if Expect: is disabled due to a previous\n                            417 response */\n  BIT(use_range);\n  BIT(rangestringalloc); /* the range string is malloc()'ed */\n  BIT(done); /* set to FALSE when Curl_init_do() is called and set to TRUE\n                when multi_done() is called, to prevent multi_done() to get\n                invoked twice when the multi interface is used. */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n  BIT(cookie_engine);\n#endif\n  BIT(prefer_ascii);   /* ASCII rather than binary */\n#ifdef CURL_LIST_ONLY_PROTOCOL\n  BIT(list_only);      /* list directory contents */\n#endif\n  BIT(wildcard_resolve); /* Set to true if any resolve change is a wildcard */\n  BIT(upload);         /* upload request */\n  BIT(internal); /* internal: true if this easy handle was created for\n                    internal use and the user does not have ownership of the\n                    handle. */\n  BIT(http_ignorecustom); /* ignore custom method from now */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  BIT(http_hd_te); /* Added HTTP header TE: */\n  BIT(http_hd_upgrade); /* Added HTTP header Upgrade: */\n  BIT(http_hd_h2_settings); /* Added HTTP header H2Settings: */\n  BIT(maybe_folded);\n  BIT(leading_unfold); /* unfold started, this is the leading bytes */\n#endif\n};\n\n/*\n * This 'UserDefined' struct must only contain data that is set once to go\n * for many (perhaps) independent connections. Return CURLE_OK or CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY. */\n\n  Curl_safefree(*charp);\n\n  if(s) {\n    if(strlen(s) > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    *charp = curlx_strdup(s);\n    if(!*charp)\n      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  }\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nCURLcode Curl_setblobopt(struct curl_blob **blobp,\n                         const struct curl_blob *blob)\n{\n  /* free the previous storage at `blobp' and replace by a dynamic storage\n     copy of blob. If CURL_BLOB_COPY is set, the data is copied. */\n\n  Curl_safefree(*blobp);\n\n  if(blob) {\n    struct curl_blob *nblob;\n    if(!blob->len || (blob->len > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    nblob = (struct curl_blob *)\n      curlx_malloc(sizeof(struct curl_blob) +\n                   ((blob->flags & CURL_BLOB_COPY) ? blob->len : 0));\n    if(!nblob)\n      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    *nblob = *blob;\n    if(blob->flags & CURL_BLOB_COPY) {\n      /* put the data after the blob struct in memory */\n      nblob->data = (char *)nblob + sizeof(struct curl_blob);\n      memcpy(nblob->data, blob->data, blob->len);\n    }\n\n    *blobp = nblob;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setstropt_userpwd(const char *option, char **userp,\n                                  char **passwdp)\n{\n  char *user = NULL;\n  char *passwd = NULL;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(userp);\n  DEBUGASSERT(passwdp);\n\n  /* Parse the login details if specified. It not then we treat NULL as a hint\n     to clear the existing data */\n  if(option) {\n    size_t len = strlen(option);\n    CURLcode result;\n    if(len > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    result = Curl_parse_login_details(option, len, &user, &passwd, NULL);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n  }\n\n  curlx_free(*userp);\n  *userp = user;\n\n  curlx_free(*passwdp);\n  *passwdp = passwd;\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setstropt_interface(char *option, char **devp,\n                                    char **ifacep, char **hostp)\n{\n  char *dev = NULL;\n  char *iface = NULL;\n  char *host = NULL;\n  CURLcode result;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(devp);\n  DEBUGASSERT(ifacep);\n  DEBUGASSERT(hostp);\n\n  if(option) {\n    /* Parse the interface details if set, otherwise clear them all */\n    result = Curl_parse_interface(option, &dev, &iface, &host);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n  }\n  curlx_free(*devp);\n  *devp = dev;\n\n  curlx_free(*ifacep);\n  *ifacep = iface;\n\n  curlx_free(*hostp);\n  *hostp = host;\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n#define C_SSLVERSION_VALUE(x)     ((x) & 0xffff)\n#define C_SSLVERSION_MAX_VALUE(x) ((unsigned long)(x) & 0xffff0000)\n#endif\n\nstatic CURLcode protocol2num(const char *str, curl_prot_t *val)\n{\n  /*\n   * We are asked to cherry-pick protocols, so play it safe and disallow all\n   * protocols to start with, and re-add the wanted ones back in.\n   */\n  *val = 0;\n\n  if(!str)\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n  if(curl_strequal(str, \"all\")) {\n    *val = ~(curl_prot_t)0;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  do {\n    const char *token = str;\n    size_t tlen;\n\n    str = strchr(str, ',');\n    tlen = str ? (size_t)(str - token) : strlen(token);\n    if(tlen) {\n      const struct Curl_scheme *h = Curl_getn_scheme(token, tlen);\n\n      if(!h || !h->run)\n        return CURLE_UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL;\n\n      *val |= h->protocol;\n    }\n  } while(str && str++);\n\n  if(!*val)\n    /* no protocol listed */\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_PROXY)\nstatic CURLcode httpauth(struct Curl_easy *data, bool proxy,\n                         unsigned long auth)\n{\n  if(auth != CURLAUTH_NONE) {\n    int bitcheck = 0;\n    bool authbits = FALSE;\n    /* the DIGEST_IE bit is only used to set a special marker, for all the\n       rest we need to handle it as normal DIGEST */\n    bool iestyle = !!(auth & CURLAUTH_DIGEST_IE);\n    if(proxy)\n      data->state.authproxy.iestyle = iestyle;\n    else\n      data->state.authhost.iestyle = iestyle;\n\n    if(auth & CURLAUTH_DIGEST_IE) {\n      auth |= CURLAUTH_DIGEST; /* set standard digest bit */\n      auth &= ~CURLAUTH_DIGEST_IE; /* unset ie digest bit */\n    }\n\n    /* switch off bits we cannot support */\n#ifndef USE_NTLM\n    auth &= ~CURLAUTH_NTLM; /* no NTLM support */\n#endif\n#ifndef USE_SPNEGO\n    auth &= ~CURLAUTH_NEGOTIATE; /* no Negotiate (SPNEGO) auth without GSS-API\n                                    or SSPI */\n#endif\n\n    /* check if any auth bit lower than CURLAUTH_ONLY is still set */\n    while(bitcheck < 31) {\n      if(auth & (1UL << bitcheck++)) {\n        authbits = TRUE;\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n    if(!authbits)\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN; /* no supported types left! */\n  }\n  if(proxy)\n    data->set.proxyauth = (uint32_t)auth;\n  else\n    data->set.httpauth = (uint32_t)auth;\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_HTTP || !CURL_DISABLE_PROXY */\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\nstatic CURLcode setopt_HTTP_VERSION(struct Curl_easy *data, long arg)\n{\n  /*\n   * This sets a requested HTTP version to be used. The value is one of\n   * the listed enums in curl/curl.h.\n   */\n  switch(arg) {\n  case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_NONE:\n    /* accepted */\n    break;\n  case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_0:\n  case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_1:\n    /* accepted */\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_HTTP2\n  case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2_0:\n  case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2TLS:\n  case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2_PRIOR_KNOWLEDGE:\n    /* accepted */\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n  case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_3:\n  case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_3ONLY:\n    /* accepted */\n    break;\n#endif\n  default:\n    /* not accepted */\n    if(arg < CURL_HTTP_VERSION_NONE)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    return CURLE_UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL;\n  }\n  data->set.httpwant = (unsigned char)arg;\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_HTTP */\n\n#ifdef USE_SSL\nCURLcode Curl_setopt_SSLVERSION(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                                long arg)\n{\n  /*\n   * Set explicit SSL version to try to connect with, as some SSL\n   * implementations are lame.\n   */\n  {\n    long version, version_max;\n    struct ssl_primary_config *primary = &data->set.ssl.primary;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n    if(option != CURLOPT_SSLVERSION)\n      primary = &data->set.proxy_ssl.primary;\n#else\n    if(option) {}\n#endif\n    version = C_SSLVERSION_VALUE(arg);\n    version_max = (long)C_SSLVERSION_MAX_VALUE(arg);\n\n    if(version < CURL_SSLVERSION_DEFAULT ||\n       version == CURL_SSLVERSION_SSLv2 ||\n       version == CURL_SSLVERSION_SSLv3 ||\n       version >= CURL_SSLVERSION_LAST ||\n       version_max < CURL_SSLVERSION_MAX_NONE ||\n       version_max >= CURL_SSLVERSION_MAX_LAST)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    if(version == CURL_SSLVERSION_DEFAULT)\n      version = CURL_SSLVERSION_TLSv1_2;\n\n    primary->version = (unsigned char)version;\n    primary->version_max = (unsigned int)version_max;\n  }\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n#endif /* !USE_SSL */\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\nstatic CURLcode setopt_RTSP_REQUEST(struct Curl_easy *data, long arg)\n{\n  /*\n   * Set the RTSP request method (OPTIONS, SETUP, PLAY, etc...)\n   * Would this be better if the RTSPREQ_* were just moved into here?\n   */\n  Curl_RtspReq rtspreq = RTSPREQ_NONE;\n  switch(arg) {\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_OPTIONS:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_OPTIONS;\n    break;\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_DESCRIBE:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_DESCRIBE;\n    break;\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_ANNOUNCE:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_ANNOUNCE;\n    break;\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_SETUP:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_SETUP;\n    break;\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_PLAY:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_PLAY;\n    break;\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_PAUSE:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_PAUSE;\n    break;\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_TEARDOWN:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_TEARDOWN;\n    break;\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_GET_PARAMETER:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_GET_PARAMETER;\n    break;\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_SET_PARAMETER:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_SET_PARAMETER;\n    break;\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_RECORD:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_RECORD;\n    break;\n  case CURL_RTSPREQ_RECEIVE:\n    rtspreq = RTSPREQ_RECEIVE;\n    break;\n  default:\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n  }\n\n  data->set.rtspreq = rtspreq;\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_RTSP */\n\n#ifdef USE_SSL\nstatic void set_ssl_options(struct ssl_config_data *ssl,\n                            struct ssl_primary_config *config,\n                            long arg)\n{\n  config->ssl_options = (unsigned char)(arg & 0xff);\n  ssl->enable_beast = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_ALLOW_BEAST);\n  ssl->no_revoke = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NO_REVOKE);\n  ssl->no_partialchain = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NO_PARTIALCHAIN);\n  ssl->revoke_best_effort = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_REVOKE_BEST_EFFORT);\n  ssl->native_ca_store = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NATIVE_CA);\n  ssl->auto_client_cert = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_AUTO_CLIENT_CERT);\n  ssl->earlydata = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_EARLYDATA);\n}\n#endif\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_long_bool(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                                 long arg)\n{\n  bool enabled = !!arg;\n  int ok = 1;\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_FORBID_REUSE:\n    /*\n     * When this transfer is done, it must not be left to be reused by a\n     * subsequent transfer but shall be closed immediately.\n     */\n    s->reuse_forbid = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FRESH_CONNECT:\n    /*\n     * This transfer shall not use a previously cached connection but\n     * should be made with a fresh new connect!\n     */\n    s->reuse_fresh = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_VERBOSE:\n    /*\n     * Verbose means infof() calls that give a lot of information about\n     * the connection and transfer procedures as well as internal choices.\n     */\n    s->verbose = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HEADER:\n    /*\n     * Set to include the header in the general data output stream.\n     */\n    s->include_header = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NOPROGRESS:\n    /*\n     * Shut off the internal supported progress meter\n     */\n    data->progress.hide = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NOBODY:\n    /*\n     * Do not include the body part in the output data stream.\n     */\n    s->opt_no_body = enabled;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n    if(s->opt_no_body)\n      /* in HTTP lingo, no body means using the HEAD request... */\n      s->method = HTTPREQ_HEAD;\n    else if(s->method == HTTPREQ_HEAD)\n      s->method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n#endif\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FAILONERROR:\n    /*\n     * Do not output the >=400 error code HTML-page, but instead only\n     * return error.\n     */\n    s->http_fail_on_error = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_KEEP_SENDING_ON_ERROR:\n    s->http_keep_sending_on_error = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_UPLOAD:\n  case CURLOPT_PUT:\n    /*\n     * We want to sent data to the remote host. If this is HTTP, that equals\n     * using the PUT request.\n     */\n    if(enabled) {\n      /* If this is HTTP, PUT is what's needed to \"upload\" */\n      s->method = HTTPREQ_PUT;\n      s->opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n    }\n    else\n      /* In HTTP, the opposite of upload is GET (unless NOBODY is true as\n         then this can be changed to HEAD later on) */\n      s->method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FILETIME:\n    /*\n     * Try to get the file time of the remote document. The time will\n     * later (possibly) become available using curl_easy_getinfo().\n     */\n    s->get_filetime = enabled;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP09_ALLOWED:\n    s->http09_allowed = enabled;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n  case CURLOPT_COOKIESESSION:\n    /*\n     * Set this option to TRUE to start a new \"cookie session\". It will\n     * prevent the forthcoming read-cookies-from-file actions to accept\n     * cookies that are marked as being session cookies, as they belong to a\n     * previous session.\n     */\n    s->cookiesession = enabled;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_AUTOREFERER:\n    /*\n     * Switch on automatic referer that gets set if curl follows locations.\n     */\n    s->http_auto_referer = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TRANSFER_ENCODING:\n    s->http_transfer_encoding = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_UNRESTRICTED_AUTH:\n    /*\n     * Send authentication (user+password) when following locations, even when\n     * hostname changed.\n     */\n    s->allow_auth_to_other_hosts = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP_TRANSFER_DECODING:\n    /*\n     * disable libcurl transfer encoding is used\n     */\n    s->http_te_skip = !enabled; /* reversed */\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP_CONTENT_DECODING:\n    /*\n     * raw data passed to the application when content encoding is used\n     */\n    s->http_ce_skip = !enabled; /* reversed */\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPGET:\n    /*\n     * Set to force us do HTTP GET\n     */\n    if(enabled) {\n      s->method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n      s->opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n    }\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_POST:\n    /* Does this option serve a purpose anymore? Yes it does, when\n       CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS is not used and the POST data is read off the\n       callback! */\n    if(enabled) {\n      s->method = HTTPREQ_POST;\n      s->opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n    }\n    else\n      s->method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_HTTP */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPPROXYTUNNEL:\n    /*\n     * Tunnel operations through the proxy instead of normal proxy use\n     */\n    s->tunnel_thru_httpproxy = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HAPROXYPROTOCOL:\n    /*\n     * Set to send the HAProxy Proxy Protocol header\n     */\n    s->haproxyprotocol = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_VERIFYPEER:\n    /*\n     * Enable peer SSL verifying for proxy.\n     */\n    s->proxy_ssl.primary.verifypeer = enabled;\n\n    /* Update the current connection proxy_ssl_config. */\n    Curl_ssl_conn_config_update(data, TRUE);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_VERIFYHOST:\n    /*\n     * Enable verification of the hostname in the peer certificate for proxy\n     */\n    s->proxy_ssl.primary.verifyhost = enabled;\n    ok = 2;\n    /* Update the current connection proxy_ssl_config. */\n    Curl_ssl_conn_config_update(data, TRUE);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TRANSFER_MODE:\n    /*\n     * set transfer mode (;type=<a|i>) when doing FTP via an HTTP proxy\n     */\n    s->proxy_transfer_mode = enabled;\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_PROXY */\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKS5_GSSAPI_NEC:\n    /*\n     * Set flag for NEC SOCK5 support\n     */\n    s->socks5_gssapi_nec = enabled;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifdef CURL_LIST_ONLY_PROTOCOL\n  case CURLOPT_DIRLISTONLY:\n    /*\n     * An option that changes the command to one that asks for a list only, no\n     * file info details. Used for FTP, POP3 and SFTP.\n     */\n    s->list_only = enabled;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_APPEND:\n    /*\n     * We want to upload and append to an existing file. Used for FTP and\n     * SFTP.\n     */\n    s->remote_append = enabled;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPRT:\n    s->ftp_use_eprt = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPSV:\n    s->ftp_use_epsv = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_USE_PRET:\n    s->ftp_use_pret = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_SKIP_PASV_IP:\n    /*\n     * Enable or disable FTP_SKIP_PASV_IP, which will disable/enable the\n     * bypass of the IP address in PASV responses.\n     */\n    s->ftp_skip_ip = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_WILDCARDMATCH:\n    s->wildcard_enabled = enabled;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_CRLF:\n    /*\n     * Kludgy option to enable CRLF conversions. Subject for removal.\n     */\n    s->crlf = enabled;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TFTP\n  case CURLOPT_TFTP_NO_OPTIONS:\n    /*\n     * Option that prevents libcurl from sending TFTP option requests to the\n     * server.\n     */\n    s->tftp_no_options = enabled;\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_TFTP */\n  case CURLOPT_TRANSFERTEXT:\n    /*\n     * This option was previously named 'FTPASCII'. Renamed to work with\n     * more protocols than merely FTP.\n     *\n     * Transfer using ASCII (instead of BINARY).\n     */\n    s->prefer_ascii = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER:\n    /*\n     * Enable peer SSL verifying.\n     */\n    s->ssl.primary.verifypeer = enabled;\n\n    /* Update the current connection ssl_config. */\n    Curl_ssl_conn_config_update(data, FALSE);\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_SSL_VERIFYPEER:\n    /*\n     * Enable peer SSL verifying for DoH.\n     */\n    s->doh_verifypeer = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_SSL_VERIFYHOST:\n    /*\n     * Enable verification of the hostname in the peer certificate for DoH\n     */\n    s->doh_verifyhost = enabled;\n    ok = 2;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_SSL_VERIFYSTATUS:\n    /*\n     * Enable certificate status verifying for DoH.\n     */\n    if(!Curl_ssl_cert_status_request())\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n\n    s->doh_verifystatus = enabled;\n    ok = 2;\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_DOH */\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST:\n    /*\n     * Enable verification of the hostname in the peer certificate\n     */\n\n    /* Obviously people are not reading documentation and too many thought\n       this argument took a boolean when it was not and misused it.\n       Treat 1 and 2 the same */\n    s->ssl.primary.verifyhost = enabled;\n    ok = 2;\n\n    /* Update the current connection ssl_config. */\n    Curl_ssl_conn_config_update(data, FALSE);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYSTATUS:\n    /*\n     * Enable certificate status verifying.\n     */\n    if(!Curl_ssl_cert_status_request())\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n\n    s->ssl.primary.verifystatus = enabled;\n\n    /* Update the current connection ssl_config. */\n    Curl_ssl_conn_config_update(data, FALSE);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CERTINFO:\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CERTINFO))\n      s->ssl.certinfo = enabled;\n    else\n#endif\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NOSIGNAL:\n    /*\n     * The application asks not to set any signal() or alarm() handlers,\n     * even when using a timeout.\n     */\n    s->no_signal = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_NODELAY:\n    /*\n     * Enable or disable TCP_NODELAY, which will disable/enable the Nagle\n     * algorithm\n     */\n    s->tcp_nodelay = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_IGNORE_CONTENT_LENGTH:\n    s->ignorecl = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_SESSIONID_CACHE:\n    s->ssl.primary.cache_session = enabled;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n    s->proxy_ssl.primary.cache_session = s->ssl.primary.cache_session;\n#endif\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_COMPRESSION:\n    s->ssh_compression = enabled;\n    break;\n#endif /* !USE_SSH */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_RCPT_ALLOWFAILS:\n    /* allow RCPT TO command to fail for some recipients */\n    s->mail_rcpt_allowfails = enabled;\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_SMTP */\n  case CURLOPT_SASL_IR:\n    /* Enable/disable SASL initial response */\n    s->sasl_ir = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_KEEPALIVE:\n    s->tcp_keepalive = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_FASTOPEN:\n#if defined(CONNECT_DATA_IDEMPOTENT) || defined(MSG_FASTOPEN) || \\\n  defined(TCP_FASTOPEN_CONNECT)\n    s->tcp_fastopen = enabled;\n    break;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_ENABLE_ALPN:\n    s->ssl_enable_alpn = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PATH_AS_IS:\n    s->path_as_is = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PIPEWAIT:\n    s->pipewait = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SUPPRESS_CONNECT_HEADERS:\n    s->suppress_connect_headers = enabled;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SHUFFLE_DNS\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_SHUFFLE_ADDRESSES:\n    s->dns_shuffle_addresses = enabled;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_DISALLOW_USERNAME_IN_URL:\n    s->disallow_username_in_url = enabled;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT:\n    s->quick_exit = enabled;\n    break;\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  if((arg > ok) || (arg < 0))\n    /* reserve other values for future use */\n    infof(data, \"boolean setopt(%d) got unsupported argument %ld,\"\n          \" treated as %d\", option, arg, enabled);\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode value_range(long *value, long below_error, long min, long max)\n{\n  if(*value < below_error)\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n  else if(*value < min)\n    *value = min;\n  else if(*value > max)\n    *value = max;\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_long_net(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                                long arg)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_CACHE_TIMEOUT:\n    if(arg != -1)\n      return setopt_set_timeout_sec(&s->dns_cache_timeout_ms, arg);\n    s->dns_cache_timeout_ms = -1;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAXCONNECTS:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 1, 1, INT_MAX);\n    if(!result)\n      s->maxconnects = (uint32_t)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SERVER_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_sec(&s->server_response_timeout, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_SERVER_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_MS:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_ms(&s->server_response_timeout, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_LOW_SPEED_LIMIT:\n    if(arg < 0)\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->low_speed_limit = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_LOW_SPEED_TIME:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 0, 0, USHRT_MAX);\n    if(!result)\n      s->low_speed_time = (uint16_t)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PORT:\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 65535))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->use_port = (unsigned short)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TIMEOUT:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_sec(&s->timeout, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_TIMEOUT_MS:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_ms(&s->timeout, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_sec(&s->connecttimeout, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT_MS:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_ms(&s->connecttimeout, arg);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_BINDLOCAL\n  case CURLOPT_LOCALPORT:\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 65535))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->localport = curlx_sltous(arg);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_LOCALPORTRANGE:\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 65535))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->localportrange = curlx_sltous(arg);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_BUFFERSIZE:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 0, READBUFFER_MIN, READBUFFER_MAX);\n    if(!result)\n      s->buffer_size = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_UPLOAD_BUFFERSIZE:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 0, UPLOADBUFFER_MIN, UPLOADBUFFER_MAX);\n    if(!result)\n      s->upload_buffer_size = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAXFILESIZE:\n    if(arg < 0)\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->max_filesize = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_IPRESOLVE:\n    if((arg < CURL_IPRESOLVE_WHATEVER) || (arg > CURL_IPRESOLVE_V6))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->ipver = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECT_ONLY:\n    if(arg < 0 || arg > 2)\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else {\n      s->connect_only = !!arg;\n      s->connect_only_ws = (arg == 2);\n    }\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_IPV6\n  case CURLOPT_ADDRESS_SCOPE:\n#if SIZEOF_LONG > 4\n    if((unsigned long)arg > UINT_MAX)\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n#endif\n    s->scope_id = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_KEEPIDLE:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 0, 0, INT_MAX);\n    if(!result)\n      s->tcp_keepidle = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_KEEPINTVL:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 0, 0, INT_MAX);\n    if(!result)\n      s->tcp_keepintvl = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_KEEPCNT:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 0, 0, INT_MAX);\n    if(!result)\n      s->tcp_keepcnt = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HAPPY_EYEBALLS_TIMEOUT_MS:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_ms(&s->happy_eyeballs_timeout, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_UPKEEP_INTERVAL_MS:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_ms(&s->upkeep_interval_ms, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_MAXAGE_CONN:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_sec(&s->conn_max_idle_ms, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_MAXLIFETIME_CONN:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_sec(&s->conn_max_age_ms, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_USE_GLOBAL_CACHE:\n    /* deprecated */\n    break;\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_long_ssl(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                                long arg)\n{\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_CA_CACHE_TIMEOUT:\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CA_CACHE)) {\n      result = value_range(&arg, -1, -1, INT_MAX);\n      if(!result)\n        s->general_ssl.ca_cache_timeout = (int)arg;\n    }\n    else\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSLVERSION:\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLVERSION:\n#endif\n    return Curl_setopt_SSLVERSION(data, option, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_FALSESTART:\n    result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_USE_SSL:\n    if((arg < CURLUSESSL_NONE) || (arg >= CURLUSESSL_LAST))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->use_ssl = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_OPTIONS:\n    set_ssl_options(&s->ssl, &s->ssl.primary, arg);\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_OPTIONS:\n    set_ssl_options(&s->proxy_ssl, &s->proxy_ssl.primary, arg);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_ENABLE_NPN:\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSLENGINE_DEFAULT:\n    Curl_safefree(s->str[STRING_SSL_ENGINE]);\n    result = Curl_ssl_set_engine_default(data);\n    break;\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return result;\n#else  /* USE_SSL */\n  (void)data;\n  (void)option;\n  (void)arg;\n  return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n#endif /* !USE_SSL */\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_long_proxy(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                                  long arg)\n{\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYPORT:\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > UINT16_MAX))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->proxyport = (uint16_t)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH:\n    return httpauth(data, TRUE, (unsigned long)arg);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYTYPE:\n    if((arg < CURLPROXY_HTTP) || (arg > CURLPROXY_SOCKS5_HOSTNAME))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->proxytype = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKS5_AUTH:\n    if(arg & ~(CURLAUTH_BASIC | CURLAUTH_GSSAPI))\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    s->socks5auth = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return CURLE_OK;\n#else\n  (void)data;\n  (void)option;\n  (void)arg;\n  return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_long_http(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                                 long arg)\n{\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION:\n    if((unsigned long)arg > 3)\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->http_follow_mode = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAXREDIRS:\n    result = value_range(&arg, -1, -1, 0x7fff);\n    if(!result)\n      s->maxredirs = (short)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_POSTREDIR:\n    if(arg < CURL_REDIR_GET_ALL)\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else {\n      s->post301 = !!(arg & CURL_REDIR_POST_301);\n      s->post302 = !!(arg & CURL_REDIR_POST_302);\n      s->post303 = !!(arg & CURL_REDIR_POST_303);\n    }\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HEADEROPT:\n    s->sep_headers = !!(arg & CURLHEADER_SEPARATE);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPAUTH:\n    return httpauth(data, FALSE, (unsigned long)arg);\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION:\n    return setopt_HTTP_VERSION(data, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_EXPECT_100_TIMEOUT_MS:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 0, 0, 0xffff);\n    if(!result)\n      s->expect_100_timeout = (unsigned short)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_WEIGHT:\n#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)\n    if((arg >= 1) && (arg <= 256))\n      s->priority.weight = (int)arg;\n    break;\n#else\n    result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#endif\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return result;\n#else\n  (void)data;\n  (void)option;\n  (void)arg;\n  return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_long_proto(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                                  long arg)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n\n  switch(option) {\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TFTP\n  case CURLOPT_TFTP_BLKSIZE:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 0, TFTP_BLKSIZE_MIN, TFTP_BLKSIZE_MAX);\n    if(!result)\n      s->tftp_blksize = (unsigned short)arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_NETRC\n  case CURLOPT_NETRC:\n    if((arg < CURL_NETRC_IGNORED) || (arg >= CURL_NETRC_LAST))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->use_netrc = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_FILEMETHOD:\n    if((arg < CURLFTPMETHOD_DEFAULT) || (arg >= CURLFTPMETHOD_LAST))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->ftp_filemethod = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_SSL_CCC:\n    if((arg < CURLFTPSSL_CCC_NONE) || (arg >= CURLFTPSSL_CCC_LAST))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->ftp_ccc = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FTPSSLAUTH:\n    if((arg < CURLFTPAUTH_DEFAULT) || (arg >= CURLFTPAUTH_LAST))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->ftpsslauth = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_ACCEPTTIMEOUT_MS:\n    return setopt_set_timeout_ms(&s->accepttimeout, arg);\n#endif\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FTP) || defined(USE_SSH)\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_CREATE_MISSING_DIRS:\n    if((arg < CURLFTP_CREATE_DIR_NONE) || (arg > CURLFTP_CREATE_DIR_RETRY))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->ftp_create_missing_dirs = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NEW_FILE_PERMS:\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 0777))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->new_file_perms = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_REQUEST:\n    return setopt_RTSP_REQUEST(data, arg);\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_CLIENT_CSEQ:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 0, 0, INT_MAX);\n    if(!result)\n      data->state.rtsp_next_client_CSeq = (uint32_t)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_SERVER_CSEQ:\n    result = value_range(&arg, 0, 0, INT_MAX);\n    if(!result)\n      data->state.rtsp_next_server_CSeq = (uint32_t)arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_AUTH_TYPES:\n    s->ssh_auth_types = (uint32_t)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NEW_DIRECTORY_PERMS:\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 0777))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      s->new_directory_perms = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_PROTOCOLS:\n    s->allowed_protocols = (curl_prot_t)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_REDIR_PROTOCOLS:\n    s->redir_protocols = (curl_prot_t)arg;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_WEBSOCKETS\n  case CURLOPT_WS_OPTIONS:\n    s->ws_raw_mode = (bool)(arg & CURLWS_RAW_MODE);\n    s->ws_no_auto_pong = (bool)(arg & CURLWS_NOAUTOPONG);\n    break;\n#endif\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_long_misc(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                                 long arg)\n{\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION:\n    if((arg < CURL_TIMECOND_NONE) || (arg >= CURL_TIMECOND_LAST))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->timecondition = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TIMEVALUE:\n    s->timevalue = (time_t)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE:\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    if(s->postfieldsize < arg &&\n       s->postfields == s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]) {\n      Curl_safefree(s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]);\n      s->postfields = NULL;\n    }\n    s->postfieldsize = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_INFILESIZE:\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->filesize = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_RESUME_FROM:\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->set_resume_from = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_UPLOAD_FLAGS:\n    s->upload_flags = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_MIME\n  case CURLOPT_MIME_OPTIONS:\n    s->mime_formescape = !!(arg & CURLMIMEOPT_FORMESCAPE);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n  case CURLOPT_HSTS_CTRL:\n    if(arg & CURLHSTS_ENABLE) {\n      if(!data->hsts) {\n        data->hsts = Curl_hsts_init();\n        if(!data->hsts)\n          return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      }\n    }\n    else\n      Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_ALTSVC\n  case CURLOPT_ALTSVC_CTRL:\n    return Curl_altsvc_ctrl(data, arg);\n#endif\n#ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n  case CURLOPT_GSSAPI_DELEGATION:\n    s->gssapi_delegation = (unsigned char)arg &\n      (CURLGSSAPI_DELEGATION_POLICY_FLAG | CURLGSSAPI_DELEGATION_FLAG);\n    break;\n#endif\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_long(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                            long arg)\n{\n  typedef CURLcode (*setoptfunc)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                 CURLoption option, long arg);\n  static const setoptfunc setopt_call[] = {\n    setopt_long_bool,\n    setopt_long_net,\n    setopt_long_http,\n    setopt_long_proxy,\n    setopt_long_ssl,\n    setopt_long_proto,\n    setopt_long_misc\n  };\n  size_t i;\n\n  for(i = 0; i < CURL_ARRAYSIZE(setopt_call); i++) {\n    CURLcode result = setopt_call[i](data, option, arg);\n    if(result != CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION)\n      return result;\n  }\n  return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_slist(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                             struct curl_slist *slist)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n  switch(option) {\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYHEADER:\n    /*\n     * Set a list with proxy headers to use (or replace internals with)\n     *\n     * Since CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER was the only way to set HTTP headers for a\n     * long time we remain doing it this way until CURLOPT_PROXYHEADER is\n     * used. As soon as this option has been used, if set to anything but\n     * NULL, custom headers for proxies are only picked from this list.\n     *\n     * Set this option to NULL to restore the previous behavior.\n     */\n    s->proxyheaders = slist;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP200ALIASES:\n    /*\n     * Set a list of aliases for HTTP 200 in response header\n     */\n    s->http200aliases = slist;\n    break;\n#endif\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FTP) || defined(USE_SSH)\n  case CURLOPT_POSTQUOTE:\n    /*\n     * List of RAW FTP commands to use after a transfer\n     */\n    s->postquote = slist;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PREQUOTE:\n    /*\n     * List of RAW FTP commands to use prior to RETR (Wesley Laxton)\n     */\n    s->prequote = slist;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_QUOTE:\n    /*\n     * List of RAW FTP commands to use before a transfer\n     */\n    s->quote = slist;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_RESOLVE:\n    /*\n     * List of HOST:PORT:[addresses] strings to populate the DNS cache with\n     * Entries added this way will remain in the cache until explicitly\n     * removed or the handle is cleaned up.\n     *\n     * Prefix the HOST with plus sign (+) to have the entry expire just like\n     * automatically added entries.\n     *\n     * Prefix the HOST with dash (-) to _remove_ the entry from the cache.\n     *\n     * This API can remove any entry from the DNS cache, but only entries\n     * that are not actually in use right now will be pruned immediately.\n     */\n    s->resolve = slist;\n    data->state.resolve = s->resolve;\n    break;\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_MIME)\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER:\n    /*\n     * Set a list with HTTP headers to use (or replace internals with)\n     */\n    s->headers = slist;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TELNET\n  case CURLOPT_TELNETOPTIONS:\n    /*\n     * Set a linked list of telnet options\n     */\n    s->telnet_options = slist;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_RCPT:\n    /* Set the list of mail recipients */\n    s->mail_rcpt = slist;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECT_TO:\n    s->connect_to = slist;\n    break;\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_SMTP) ||       \\\n  !defined(CURL_DISABLE_IMAP)\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_MIME\nstatic CURLcode setopt_mimepost(struct Curl_easy *data, curl_mime *mimep)\n{\n  /*\n   * Set to make us do MIME POST\n   */\n  CURLcode result;\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n  if(!s->mimepostp) {\n    s->mimepostp = curlx_malloc(sizeof(*s->mimepostp));\n    if(!s->mimepostp)\n      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    Curl_mime_initpart(s->mimepostp);\n  }\n\n  result = Curl_mime_set_subparts(s->mimepostp, mimep, FALSE);\n  if(!result) {\n    s->method = HTTPREQ_POST_MIME;\n    s->opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FORM_API\n    Curl_mime_cleanpart(data->state.formp);\n    Curl_safefree(data->state.formp);\n    data->state.mimepost = NULL;\n#endif\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_MIME */\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_HTTP || !CURL_DISABLE_SMTP || !CURL_DISABLE_IMAP */\n\n/* assorted pointer type arguments */\nstatic CURLcode setopt_pointers(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                                va_list param)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n  switch(option) {\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FORM_API\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPPOST:\n    /*\n     * Set to make us do HTTP POST. Legacy API-style.\n     */\n    s->httppost = va_arg(param, struct curl_httppost *);\n    s->method = HTTPREQ_POST_FORM;\n    s->opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n    Curl_mime_cleanpart(data->state.formp);\n    Curl_safefree(data->state.formp);\n    data->state.mimepost = NULL;\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_FORM_API */\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_HTTP */\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_SMTP) ||       \\\n  !defined(CURL_DISABLE_IMAP)\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_MIME\n  case CURLOPT_MIMEPOST:\n    result = setopt_mimepost(data, va_arg(param, curl_mime *));\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_MIME */\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_HTTP || !CURL_DISABLE_SMTP || !CURL_DISABLE_IMAP */\n  case CURLOPT_STDERR:\n    /*\n     * Set to a FILE * that should receive all error writes. This\n     * defaults to stderr for normal operations.\n     */\n    s->err = va_arg(param, FILE *);\n    if(!s->err)\n      s->err = stderr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SHARE: {\n    struct Curl_share *set = va_arg(param, struct Curl_share *);\n\n    /* disconnect from old share, if any */\n    if(data->share) {\n      Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES)\n      if(data->share->cookies == data->cookies)\n        data->cookies = NULL;\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n      if(data->share->hsts == data->hsts)\n        data->hsts = NULL;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n      if(data->psl == &data->share->psl)\n        data->psl = data->multi ? &data->multi->psl : NULL;\n#endif\n      if(data->share->specifier & (1 << CURL_LOCK_DATA_DNS)) {\n        Curl_resolv_unlink(data, &data->state.dns[0]);\n        Curl_resolv_unlink(data, &data->state.dns[1]);\n      }\n\n      data->share->dirty--;\n\n      Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE);\n      data->share = NULL;\n    }\n\n    if(GOOD_SHARE_HANDLE(set))\n      /* use new share if it set */\n      data->share = set;\n    if(data->share) {\n\n      Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n\n      data->share->dirty++;\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES)\n      if(data->share->cookies) {\n        /* use shared cookie list, first free own one if any */\n        Curl_cookie_cleanup(data->cookies);\n        /* enable cookies since we now use a share that uses cookies! */\n        data->cookies = data->share->cookies;\n      }\n#endif /* CURL_DISABLE_HTTP */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n      if(data->share->hsts) {\n        /* first free the private one if any */\n        Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n        data->hsts = data->share->hsts;\n      }\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n      if(data->share->specifier & (1 << CURL_LOCK_DATA_PSL))\n        data->psl = &data->share->psl;\n#endif\n\n      Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE);\n    }\n    /* check for host cache not needed,\n     * it will be done by curl_easy_perform */\n    break;\n  }\n\n#ifdef USE_HTTP2\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS:\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E: {\n    struct Curl_easy *dep = va_arg(param, struct Curl_easy *);\n    if(!dep || GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(dep))\n      return Curl_data_priority_add_child(dep, data,\n                                          option == CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E);\n    break;\n  }\n#endif\n\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\nstatic CURLcode cookielist(struct Curl_easy *data, const char *ptr)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  if(!ptr)\n    return CURLE_OK;\n\n  if(curl_strequal(ptr, \"ALL\")) {\n    /* clear all cookies */\n    Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n    Curl_cookie_clearall(data->cookies);\n    Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE);\n  }\n  else if(curl_strequal(ptr, \"SESS\")) {\n    /* clear session cookies */\n    Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n    Curl_cookie_clearsess(data->cookies);\n    Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE);\n  }\n  else if(curl_strequal(ptr, \"FLUSH\")) {\n    /* flush cookies to file, takes care of the locking */\n    Curl_flush_cookies(data, FALSE);\n  }\n  else if(curl_strequal(ptr, \"RELOAD\")) {\n    /* reload cookies from file */\n    return Curl_cookie_loadfiles(data);\n  }\n  else {\n    if(!data->cookies) {\n      /* if cookie engine was not running,
