activate it */\n      data->cookies = Curl_cookie_init();\n      if(!data->cookies)\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      data->state.cookie_engine = TRUE;\n    }\n\n    /* general protection against mistakes and abuse */\n    if(strlen(ptr) > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n    if(checkprefix(\"Set-Cookie:\", ptr))\n      /* HTTP Header format line */\n      result = Curl_cookie_add(data, data->cookies, TRUE, FALSE, ptr + 11,\n                               NULL, NULL, TRUE);\n    else\n      /* Netscape format line */\n      result = Curl_cookie_add(data, data->cookies, FALSE, FALSE, ptr, NULL,\n                               NULL, TRUE);\n    Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE);\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cookiefile(struct Curl_easy *data, const char *ptr)\n{\n  /*\n   * Set cookie file to read and parse. Can be used multiple times.\n   */\n  if(ptr) {\n    struct curl_slist *cl;\n    /* general protection against mistakes and abuse */\n    if(strlen(ptr) > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    /* append the cookie filename to the list of filenames, and deal with\n       them later */\n    cl = curl_slist_append(data->state.cookielist, ptr);\n    if(!cl) {\n      curl_slist_free_all(data->state.cookielist);\n      data->state.cookielist = NULL;\n      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    }\n    data->state.cookielist = cl; /* store the list for later use */\n  }\n  else {\n    /* clear the list of cookie files */\n    curl_slist_free_all(data->state.cookielist);\n    data->state.cookielist = NULL;\n\n    if(!data->share || !data->share->cookies) {\n      /* throw away all existing cookies if this is not a shared cookie\n         container */\n      Curl_cookie_clearall(data->cookies);\n      Curl_cookie_cleanup(data->cookies);\n    }\n    /* disable the cookie engine */\n    data->cookies = NULL;\n  }\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\nstatic CURLcode setopt_cptr_proxy(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                                  const char *ptr)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYUSERPWD: {\n    /*\n     * user:password needed to use the proxy\n     */\n    char *u = NULL;\n    char *p = NULL;\n    result = setstropt_userpwd(ptr, &u, &p);\n\n    /* URL decode the components */\n    if(!result && u) {\n      Curl_safefree(s->str[STRING_PROXYUSERNAME]);\n      result = Curl_urldecode(u, 0, &s->str[STRING_PROXYUSERNAME], NULL,\n                              REJECT_ZERO);\n    }\n    if(!result && p) {\n      Curl_safefree(s->str[STRING_PROXYPASSWORD]);\n      result = Curl_urldecode(p, 0, &s->str[STRING_PROXYPASSWORD], NULL,\n                              REJECT_ZERO);\n    }\n    curlx_free(u);\n    curlx_free(p);\n    break;\n  }\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYUSERNAME:\n    /*\n     * authentication username to use in the operation\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_PROXYUSERNAME], ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYPASSWORD:\n    /*\n     * authentication password to use in the operation\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_PROXYPASSWORD], ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_NOPROXY:\n    /*\n     * proxy exception list\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_NOPROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERT:\n    /*\n     * String that holds filename of the SSL certificate to use for proxy\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_CERT_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERTTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL certificate to use for proxy\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_CERT_TYPE_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLKEY:\n    /*\n     * String that holds filename of the SSL key to use for proxy\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_KEY_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_KEYPASSWD:\n    /*\n     * String that holds the SSL private key password for proxy.\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_KEY_PASSWD_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLKEYTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL key to use for proxy\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_KEY_TYPE_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_CIPHER_LIST:\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CIPHER_LIST)) {\n      /* set a list of cipher we want to use in the SSL connection for proxy */\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER_LIST_PROXY], ptr);\n    }\n    else\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLS13_CIPHERS:\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_TLS13_CIPHERSUITES))\n      /* set preferred list of TLS 1.3 cipher suites for proxy */\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER13_LIST_PROXY], ptr);\n    else\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY:\n    /*\n     * Set proxy server:port to use as proxy.\n     *\n     * If the proxy is set to \"\" (and CURLOPT_SOCKS_PROXY is set to \"\" or NULL)\n     * we explicitly say that we do not want to use a proxy\n     * (even though there might be environment variables saying so).\n     *\n     * Setting it to NULL, means no proxy but allows the environment variables\n     * to decide for us (if CURLOPT_SOCKS_PROXY setting it to NULL).\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PRE_PROXY:\n    /*\n     * Set proxy server:port to use as SOCKS proxy.\n     *\n     * If the proxy is set to \"\" or NULL we explicitly say that we do not want\n     * to use the socks proxy.\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_PRE_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKS5_GSSAPI_SERVICE:\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SERVICE_NAME:\n    /*\n     * Set proxy authentication service name for Kerberos 5 and SPNEGO\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_PROXY_SERVICE_NAME], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_PINNEDPUBLICKEY:\n    /*\n     * Set pinned public key for SSL connection.\n     * Specify filename of the public key in DER format.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_PINNEDPUBKEY))\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_PINNEDPUBLICKEY_PROXY], ptr);\n#endif\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n\n  case CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP:\n    /*\n     * Set the client IP to send through HAProxy PROXY protocol\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP], ptr);\n\n    /* enable the HAProxy protocol if an IP is provided */\n    s->haproxyprotocol = !!s->str[STRING_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP];\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CAINFO:\n    /*\n     * Set CA info SSL connection for proxy. Specify filename of the\n     * CA certificate\n     */\n    s->proxy_ssl.custom_cafile = TRUE;\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_CAFILE_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CRLFILE:\n    /*\n     * Set CRL file info for SSL connection for proxy. Specify filename of the\n     * CRL to check certificates revocation\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_CRLFILE_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_ISSUERCERT:\n    /*\n     * Set Issuer certificate file\n     * to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_ISSUERCERT_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CAPATH:\n    /*\n     * Set CA path info for SSL connection proxy. Specify directory name of the\n     * CA certificates which have been prepared using openssl c_rehash utility.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CA_PATH)) {\n      /* This does not work on Windows. */\n      s->proxy_ssl.custom_capath = TRUE;\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_CAPATH_PROXY], ptr);\n    }\n#endif\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n#endif\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_MQTT)\n/*\n * A string with POST data. Makes curl HTTP POST. Even if it is NULL. If\n * needed, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE must have been set prior to\n * CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS and not altered later.\n */\nstatic CURLcode setopt_copypostfields(const char *ptr, struct UserDefined *s)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  if(!ptr || s->postfieldsize == -1)\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], ptr);\n  else {\n    size_t pflen;\n\n    if(s->postfieldsize < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    pflen = curlx_sotouz_range(s->postfieldsize, 0, SIZE_MAX);\n    if(pflen == SIZE_MAX)\n      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    else {\n      /* Allocate even when size == 0. This satisfies the need of possible\n         later address compare to detect the COPYPOSTFIELDS mode, and to mark\n         that postfields is used rather than read function or form data.\n      */\n      char *p = curlx_memdup0(ptr, pflen);\n      if(!p)\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      else {\n        curlx_free(s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]);\n        s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS] = p;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  s->postfields = s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS];\n  s->method = HTTPREQ_POST;\n  return result;\n}\n#endif\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_cptr(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                            char *ptr)\n{\n  CURLcode result;\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  result = setopt_cptr_proxy(data, option, ptr);\n  if(result != CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION)\n    return result;\n#endif\n  result = CURLE_OK;\n\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_CAINFO:\n    /*\n     * Set CA info for SSL connection. Specify filename of the CA certificate\n     */\n    s->ssl.custom_cafile = TRUE;\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_CAFILE], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_CAPATH:\n    /*\n     * Set CA path info for SSL connection. Specify directory name of the CA\n     * certificates which have been prepared using openssl c_rehash utility.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CA_PATH)) {\n      /* This does not work on Windows. */\n      s->ssl.custom_capath = TRUE;\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_CAPATH], ptr);\n    }\n#endif\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_CRLFILE:\n    /*\n     * Set CRL file info for SSL connection. Specify filename of the CRL\n     * to check certificates revocation\n     */\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CRLFILE))\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_CRLFILE], ptr);\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_CIPHER_LIST:\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CIPHER_LIST))\n      /* set a list of cipher we want to use in the SSL connection */\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER_LIST], ptr);\n    else\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_TLS13_CIPHERS:\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_TLS13_CIPHERSUITES)) {\n      /* set preferred list of TLS 1.3 cipher suites */\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER13_LIST], ptr);\n    }\n    else\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_RANDOM_FILE:\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_EGDSOCKET:\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_REQUEST_TARGET:\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_TARGET], ptr);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_NETRC\n  case CURLOPT_NETRC_FILE:\n    /*\n     * Use this file instead of the $HOME/.netrc file\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_NETRC_FILE], ptr);\n#endif\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_MQTT)\n  case CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS:\n    return setopt_copypostfields(ptr, s);\n\n  case CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS:\n    /*\n     * Like above, but use static data instead of copying it.\n     */\n    s->postfields = ptr;\n    /* Release old copied data. */\n    Curl_safefree(s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]);\n    s->method = HTTPREQ_POST;\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_HTTP || !CURL_DISABLE_MQTT */\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  case CURLOPT_TRAILERDATA:\n    s->trailer_data = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_ACCEPT_ENCODING:\n    /*\n     * String to use at the value of Accept-Encoding header.\n     *\n     * If the encoding is set to \"\" we use an Accept-Encoding header that\n     * encompasses all the encodings we support.\n     * If the encoding is set to NULL we do not send an Accept-Encoding header\n     * and ignore an received Content-Encoding header.\n     *\n     */\n    if(ptr && !*ptr) {\n      ptr = Curl_get_content_encodings();\n      if(ptr) {\n        curlx_free(s->str[STRING_ENCODING]);\n        s->str[STRING_ENCODING] = ptr;\n      }\n      else\n        result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      return result;\n    }\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_ENCODING], ptr);\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_AWS\n  case CURLOPT_AWS_SIGV4:\n    /*\n     * String that is merged to some authentication\n     * parameters are used by the algorithm.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_AWS_SIGV4], ptr);\n    /*\n     * Basic been set by default it need to be unset here\n     */\n    if(s->str[STRING_AWS_SIGV4])\n      s->httpauth = CURLAUTH_AWS_SIGV4;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_REFERER:\n    /*\n     * String to set in the HTTP Referer: field.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SET_REFERER], ptr);\n    Curl_bufref_set(&data->state.referer, s->str[STRING_SET_REFERER], 0, NULL);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_USERAGENT:\n    /*\n     * String to use in the HTTP User-Agent field\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_USERAGENT], ptr);\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n  case CURLOPT_COOKIE:\n    /*\n     * Cookie string to send to the remote server in the request.\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_COOKIE], ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_COOKIEFILE:\n    return cookiefile(data, ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_COOKIEJAR:\n    /*\n     * Set cookie filename to dump all cookies to when we are done.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_COOKIEJAR], ptr);\n    if(!result) {\n      /*\n       * Activate the cookie parser. This may or may not already\n       * have been made.\n       */\n      if(!data->cookies)\n        data->cookies = Curl_cookie_init();\n      if(!data->cookies)\n        result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      else\n        data->state.cookie_engine = TRUE;\n    }\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_COOKIELIST:\n    return cookielist(data, ptr);\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES */\n\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_HTTP */\n\n  case CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST:\n    /*\n     * Set a custom string to use as request\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_CUSTOMREQUEST], ptr);\n\n    /* we do not set\n       s->method = HTTPREQ_CUSTOM;\n       here, we continue as if we were using the already set type\n       and this just changes the actual request keyword */\n  case CURLOPT_SERVICE_NAME:\n    /*\n     * Set authentication service name for DIGEST-MD5, Kerberos 5 and SPNEGO\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SERVICE_NAME], ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_HEADERDATA:\n    /*\n     * Custom pointer to pass the header write callback function\n     */\n    s->writeheader = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_READDATA:\n    /*\n     * FILE pointer to read the file to be uploaded from. Or possibly used as\n     * argument to the read callback.\n     */\n    s->in_set = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_WRITEDATA:\n    /*\n     * FILE pointer to write to. Or possibly used as argument to the write\n     * callback.\n     */\n    s->out = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DEBUGDATA:\n    /*\n     * Set to a void * that should receive all error writes. This\n     * defaults to CURLOPT_STDERR for normal operations.\n     */\n    s->debugdata = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROGRESSDATA:\n    /*\n     * Custom client data to pass to the progress callback\n     */\n    s->progress_client = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SEEKDATA:\n    /*\n     * Seek control callback. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    s->seek_client = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_IOCTLDATA:\n    /*\n     * I/O control data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    s->ioctl_client = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_CTX_DATA:\n    /*\n     * Set an SSL_CTX callback parameter pointer\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_SSL_CTX)) {\n      s->ssl.fsslctxp = ptr;\n      break;\n    }\n    else\n#endif\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKOPTDATA:\n    /*\n     * socket callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    s->sockopt_client = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_OPENSOCKETDATA:\n    /*\n     * socket callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    s->opensocket_client = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_RESOLVER_START_DATA:\n    /*\n     * resolver start callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    s->resolver_start_client = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CLOSESOCKETDATA:\n    /*\n     * socket callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    s->closesocket_client = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PREREQDATA:\n    s->prereq_userp = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_ERRORBUFFER:\n    /*\n     * Error buffer provided by the caller to get the human readable error\n     * string in.\n     */\n    s->errorbuffer = ptr;\n    break;\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  case CURLOPT_FTPPORT:\n    /*\n     * Use FTP PORT, this also specifies which IP address to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_FTPPORT], ptr);\n    s->ftp_use_port = !!(s->str[STRING_FTPPORT]);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_ACCOUNT:\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_FTP_ACCOUNT], ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_ALTERNATIVE_TO_USER:\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_FTP_ALTERNATIVE_TO_USER], ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_KRBLEVEL:\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN; /* removed in 8.17.0 */\n  case CURLOPT_CHUNK_DATA:\n    s->wildcardptr = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FNMATCH_DATA:\n    s->fnmatch_data = ptr;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_URL:\n    /*\n     * The URL to fetch.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SET_URL], ptr);\n    Curl_bufref_set(&data->state.url, s->str[STRING_SET_URL], 0, NULL);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_USERPWD:\n    /*\n     * user:password to use in the operation\n     */\n    return setstropt_userpwd(ptr, &s->str[STRING_USERNAME],\n                             &s->str[STRING_PASSWORD]);\n\n  case CURLOPT_USERNAME:\n    /*\n     * authentication username to use in the operation\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_USERNAME], ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_PASSWORD:\n    /*\n     * authentication password to use in the operation\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_PASSWORD], ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_LOGIN_OPTIONS:\n    /*\n     * authentication options to use in the operation\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_OPTIONS], ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_XOAUTH2_BEARER:\n    /*\n     * OAuth 2.0 bearer token to use in the operation\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_BEARER], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_RANGE:\n    /*\n     * What range of the file you want to transfer\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SET_RANGE], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_CURLU:\n    /*\n     * pass CURLU to set URL\n     */\n    Curl_bufref_free(&data->state.url);\n    Curl_safefree(s->str[STRING_SET_URL]);\n    s->uh = (CURLU *)ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSLCERT:\n    /*\n     * String that holds filename of the SSL certificate to use\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_CERT], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_SSLCERTTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL certificate to use\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_CERT_TYPE], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_SSLKEY:\n    /*\n     * String that holds filename of the SSL key to use\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_KEY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_SSLKEYTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL key to use\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_KEY_TYPE], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_KEYPASSWD:\n    /*\n     * String that holds the SSL or SSH private key password.\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_KEY_PASSWD], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_SSLENGINE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds the SSL crypto engine.\n     */\n    if(ptr && ptr[0]) {\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_ENGINE], ptr);\n      if(!result) {\n        result = Curl_ssl_set_engine(data, ptr);\n      }\n    }\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_INTERFACE:\n    /*\n     * Set what interface or address/hostname to bind the socket to when\n     * performing an operation and thus what from-IP your connection will use.\n     */\n    return setstropt_interface(ptr,\n                               &s->str[STRING_DEVICE],\n                               &s->str[STRING_INTERFACE],\n                               &s->str[STRING_BINDHOST]);\n  case CURLOPT_ISSUERCERT:\n    /*\n     * Set Issuer certificate file\n     * to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_ISSUERCERT))\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_ISSUERCERT], ptr);\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_PRIVATE:\n    /*\n     * Set private data pointer.\n     */\n    s->private_data = ptr;\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_EC_CURVES:\n    /*\n     * Set accepted curves in SSL connection setup.\n     * Specify colon-delimited list of curve algorithm names.\n     */\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_SSL_EC_CURVES))\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_EC_CURVES], ptr);\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS:\n    /*\n     * Set accepted signature algorithms.\n     * Specify colon-delimited list of signature scheme names.\n     */\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS))\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS], ptr);\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_PINNEDPUBLICKEY:\n    /*\n     * Set pinned public key for SSL connection.\n     * Specify filename of the public key in DER format.\n     */\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_PINNEDPUBKEY))\n      return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_PINNEDPUBLICKEY], ptr);\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_PUBLIC_KEYFILE:\n    /*\n     * Use this file instead of the $HOME/.ssh/id_dsa.pub file\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_PRIVATE_KEYFILE:\n    /*\n     * Use this file instead of the $HOME/.ssh/id_dsa file\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_KEYDATA:\n    /*\n     * Custom client data to pass to the SSH keyfunc callback\n     */\n    s->ssh_keyfunc_userp = ptr;\n    break;\n#if defined(USE_LIBSSH2) || defined(USE_LIBSSH)\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_MD5:\n    /*\n     * Option to allow for the MD5 of the host public key to be checked\n     * for validation purposes.\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_MD5], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_KNOWNHOSTS:\n    /*\n     * Store the filename to read known hosts from.\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSH_KNOWNHOSTS], ptr);\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBSSH2\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_SHA256:\n    /*\n     * Option to allow for the SHA256 of the host public key to be checked\n     * for validation purposes.\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_SHA256], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOSTKEYDATA:\n    /*\n     * Custom client data to pass to the SSH keyfunc callback\n     */\n    s->ssh_hostkeyfunc_userp = ptr;\n    break;\n#endif /* USE_LIBSSH2 */\n#endif /* USE_SSH */\n  case CURLOPT_PROTOCOLS_STR:\n    if(ptr) {\n      curl_prot_t protos;\n      result = protocol2num(ptr, &protos);\n      if(!result)\n        s->allowed_protocols = protos;\n    }\n    else\n      /* make a NULL argument reset to default */\n      s->allowed_protocols = (curl_prot_t)CURLPROTO_ALL;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_REDIR_PROTOCOLS_STR:\n    if(ptr) {\n      curl_prot_t protos;\n      result = protocol2num(ptr, &protos);\n      if(!result)\n        s->redir_protocols = protos;\n    }\n    else\n      /* make a NULL argument reset to default */\n      s->redir_protocols = (curl_prot_t)CURLPROTO_REDIR;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DEFAULT_PROTOCOL:\n    /* Set the protocol to use when the URL does not include any protocol */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_DEFAULT_PROTOCOL], ptr);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_FROM:\n    /* Set the SMTP mail originator */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_MAIL_FROM], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_AUTH:\n    /* Set the SMTP auth originator */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_MAIL_AUTH], ptr);\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SASL_AUTHZID:\n    /* Authorization identity (identity to act as) */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SASL_AUTHZID], ptr);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_SESSION_ID:\n    /*\n     * Set the RTSP Session ID manually. Useful if the application is\n     * resuming a previously established RTSP session\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_RTSP_SESSION_ID], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_STREAM_URI:\n    /*\n     * Set the Stream URI for the RTSP request. Unless the request is\n     * for generic server options, the application will need to set this.\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_RTSP_STREAM_URI], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_TRANSPORT:\n    /*\n     * The content of the Transport: header for the RTSP request\n     */\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_RTSP_TRANSPORT], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_INTERLEAVEDATA:\n    s->rtp_out = ptr;\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_RTSP */\n#ifdef USE_TLS_SRP\n  case CURLOPT_TLSAUTH_USERNAME:\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_TLSAUTH_USERNAME], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD:\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_TLSAUTH_TYPE:\n    if(ptr && !curl_strequal(ptr, \"SRP\"))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLSAUTH_USERNAME:\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_TLSAUTH_USERNAME_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD:\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD_PROXY], ptr);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLSAUTH_TYPE:\n    if(ptr && !curl_strequal(ptr, \"SRP\"))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    break;\n#endif\n#endif\n#ifdef CURLRES_ARES\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_SERVERS:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_DNS_SERVERS], ptr);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n    return Curl_async_ares_set_dns_servers(data);\n\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_INTERFACE:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_DNS_INTERFACE], ptr);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n    return Curl_async_ares_set_dns_interface(data);\n\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_LOCAL_IP4:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP4], ptr);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n    return Curl_async_ares_set_dns_local_ip4(data);\n\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_LOCAL_IP6:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP6], ptr);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n    return Curl_async_ares_set_dns_local_ip6(data);\n\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  case CURLOPT_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH:\n    s->abstract_unix_socket = FALSE;\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH], ptr);\n\n  case CURLOPT_ABSTRACT_UNIX_SOCKET:\n    s->abstract_unix_socket = TRUE;\n    return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH], ptr);\n\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_URL:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_DOH], ptr);\n    s->doh = !!(s->str[STRING_DOH]);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSREADDATA:\n    s->hsts_read_userp = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSWRITEDATA:\n    s->hsts_write_userp = ptr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTS: {\n    struct curl_slist *h;\n    if(!data->hsts) {\n      data->hsts = Curl_hsts_init();\n      if(!data->hsts)\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    }\n    if(ptr) {\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_HSTS], ptr);\n      if(result)\n        return result;\n      /* this needs to build a list of filenames to read from, so that it can\n         read them later, as we might get a shared HSTS handle to load them\n         into */\n      h = curl_slist_append(data->state.hstslist, ptr);\n      if(!h) {\n        curl_slist_free_all(data->state.hstslist);\n        data->state.hstslist = NULL;\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      }\n      data->state.hstslist = h; /* store the list for later use */\n    }\n    else {\n      /* clear the list of HSTS files */\n      curl_slist_free_all(data->state.hstslist);\n      data->state.hstslist = NULL;\n      if(!data->share || !data->share->hsts)\n        /* throw away the HSTS cache unless shared */\n        Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n    }\n    break;\n  }\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_HSTS */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_ALTSVC\n  case CURLOPT_ALTSVC:\n    if(!data->asi) {\n      data->asi = Curl_altsvc_init();\n      if(!data->asi)\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    }\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_ALTSVC], ptr);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n    if(ptr)\n      return Curl_altsvc_load(data->asi, ptr);\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_ALTSVC */\n#ifdef USE_ECH\n  case CURLOPT_ECH: {\n    size_t plen = 0;\n\n    if(!ptr) {\n      s->tls_ech = CURLECH_DISABLE;\n      break;\n    }\n    plen = strlen(ptr);\n    if(plen > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH) {\n      s->tls_ech = CURLECH_DISABLE;\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    }\n    /* set tls_ech flag value, preserving CLA_CFG bit */\n    if(!strcmp(ptr, \"false\"))\n      s->tls_ech = (s->tls_ech & CURLECH_CLA_CFG) | CURLECH_DISABLE;\n    else if(!strcmp(ptr, \"grease\"))\n      s->tls_ech = (s->tls_ech & CURLECH_CLA_CFG) | CURLECH_GREASE;\n    else if(!strcmp(ptr, \"true\"))\n      s->tls_ech = (s->tls_ech & CURLECH_CLA_CFG) | CURLECH_ENABLE;\n    else if(!strcmp(ptr, \"hard\"))\n      s->tls_ech = (s->tls_ech & CURLECH_CLA_CFG) | CURLECH_HARD;\n    else if(plen > 5 && !strncmp(ptr, \"ecl:\", 4)) {\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_ECH_CONFIG], ptr + 4);\n      if(!result)\n        s->tls_ech |= CURLECH_CLA_CFG;\n    }\n    else if(plen > 4 && !strncmp(ptr, \"pn:\", 3))\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_ECH_PUBLIC], ptr + 3);\n    break;\n  }\n#endif\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_func(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                            va_list param)\n{\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_PROGRESSFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Progress callback function\n     */\n    s->fprogress = va_arg(param, curl_progress_callback);\n    if(s->fprogress)\n      data->progress.callback = TRUE; /* no longer internal */\n    else\n      data->progress.callback = FALSE; /* NULL enforces internal */\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_XFERINFOFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Transfer info callback function\n     */\n    s->fxferinfo = va_arg(param, curl_xferinfo_callback);\n    if(s->fxferinfo)\n      data->progress.callback = TRUE; /* no longer internal */\n    else\n      data->progress.callback = FALSE; /* NULL enforces internal */\n\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DEBUGFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * stderr write callback.\n     */\n    s->fdebug = va_arg(param, curl_debug_callback);\n    /*\n     * if the callback provided is NULL, it will use the default callback\n     */\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HEADERFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Set header write callback\n     */\n    s->fwrite_header = va_arg(param, curl_write_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_WRITEFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Set data write callback\n     */\n    s->fwrite_func = va_arg(param, curl_write_callback);\n    if(!s->fwrite_func)\n#if defined(__clang__) && __clang_major__ >= 16\n#pragma clang diagnostic push\n#pragma clang diagnostic ignored \"-Wcast-function-type-strict\"\n#endif\n      /* When set to NULL, reset to our internal default function */\n      s->fwrite_func = (curl_write_callback)fwrite;\n#if defined(__clang__) && __clang_major__ >= 16\n#pragma clang diagnostic pop\n#endif\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_READFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Read data callback\n     */\n    s->fread_func_set = va_arg(param, curl_read_callback);\n    if(!s->fread_func_set) {\n      s->is_fread_set = 0;\n#if defined(__clang__) && __clang_major__ >= 16\n#pragma clang diagnostic push\n#pragma clang diagnostic ignored \"-Wcast-function-type-strict\"\n#endif\n      /* When set to NULL, reset to our internal default function */\n      s->fread_func_set = (curl_read_callback)fread;\n#if defined(__clang__) && __clang_major__ >= 16\n#pragma clang diagnostic pop\n#endif\n    }\n    else\n      s->is_fread_set = 1;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SEEKFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Seek callback. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    s->seek_func = va_arg(param, curl_seek_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_IOCTLFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * I/O control callback. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    s->ioctl_func = va_arg(param, curl_ioctl_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_CTX_FUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Set an SSL_CTX callback\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_SSL_CTX)) {\n      s->ssl.fsslctx = va_arg(param, curl_ssl_ctx_callback);\n      break;\n    }\n    else\n#endif\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKOPTFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * socket callback function: called after socket() but before connect()\n     */\n    s->fsockopt = va_arg(param, curl_sockopt_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_OPENSOCKETFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * open/create socket callback function: called instead of socket(),\n     * before connect()\n     */\n    s->fopensocket = va_arg(param, curl_opensocket_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_CLOSESOCKETFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * close socket callback function: called instead of close()\n     * when shutting down a connection\n     */\n    s->fclosesocket = va_arg(param, curl_closesocket_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RESOLVER_START_FUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * resolver start callback function: called before a new resolver request\n     * is started\n     */\n    s->resolver_start = va_arg(param, curl_resolver_start_callback);\n    break;\n\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n#ifdef USE_LIBSSH2\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOSTKEYFUNCTION:\n    /* the callback to check the hostkey without the knownhost file */\n    s->ssh_hostkeyfunc = va_arg(param, curl_sshhostkeycallback);\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_KEYFUNCTION:\n    /* setting to NULL is fine since the ssh.c functions themselves will\n       then revert to use the internal default */\n    s->ssh_keyfunc = va_arg(param, curl_sshkeycallback);\n    break;\n\n#endif /* USE_SSH */\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  case CURLOPT_INTERLEAVEFUNCTION:\n    /* Set the user defined RTP write function */\n    s->fwrite_rtp = va_arg(param, curl_write_callback);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  case CURLOPT_CHUNK_BGN_FUNCTION:\n    s->chunk_bgn = va_arg(param, curl_chunk_bgn_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CHUNK_END_FUNCTION:\n    s->chunk_end = va_arg(param, curl_chunk_end_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FNMATCH_FUNCTION:\n    s->fnmatch = va_arg(param, curl_fnmatch_callback);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  case CURLOPT_TRAILERFUNCTION:\n    s->trailer_callback = va_arg(param, curl_trailer_callback);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSREADFUNCTION:\n    s->hsts_read = va_arg(param, curl_hstsread_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSWRITEFUNCTION:\n    s->hsts_write = va_arg(param, curl_hstswrite_callback);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_PREREQFUNCTION:\n    s->fprereq = va_arg(param, curl_prereq_callback);\n    break;\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_offt(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                            curl_off_t offt)\n{\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_TIMEVALUE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * This is the value to compare with the remote document with the\n     * method set with CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION\n     */\n    s->timevalue = (time_t)offt;\n    break;\n\n    /* MQTT \"borrows\" some of the HTTP options */\n  case CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * The size of the POSTFIELD data to prevent libcurl to do strlen() to\n     * figure it out. Enables binary posts.\n     */\n    if(offt < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    if(s->postfieldsize < offt &&\n       s->postfields == s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]) {\n      /* Previous CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS is no longer valid. */\n      Curl_safefree(s->str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]);\n      s->postfields = NULL;\n    }\n    s->postfieldsize = offt;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_INFILESIZE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * If known, this should inform curl about the file size of the\n     * to-be-uploaded file.\n     */\n    if(offt < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->filesize = offt;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAX_SEND_SPEED_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * When transfer uploads are faster then CURLOPT_MAX_SEND_SPEED_LARGE\n     * bytes per second the transfer is throttled..\n     */\n    if(offt < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->max_send_speed = offt;\n    Curl_rlimit_init(&data->progress.ul.rlimit, offt, offt,\n                     Curl_pgrs_now(data));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAX_RECV_SPEED_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * When receiving data faster than CURLOPT_MAX_RECV_SPEED_LARGE bytes per\n     * second the transfer is throttled..\n     */\n    if(offt < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->max_recv_speed = offt;\n    Curl_rlimit_init(&data->progress.dl.rlimit, offt, offt,\n                     Curl_pgrs_now(data));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_RESUME_FROM_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * Resume transfer at the given file position\n     */\n    if(offt < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->set_resume_from = offt;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAXFILESIZE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * Set the maximum size of a file to download.\n     */\n    if(offt < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    s->max_filesize = offt;\n    break;\n\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setopt_blob(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option,\n                            struct curl_blob *blob)\n{\n  struct UserDefined *s = &data->set;\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_SSLCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL certificate to use\n     */\n    return Curl_setblobopt(&s->blobs[BLOB_CERT], blob);\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL certificate to use for proxy\n     */\n    return Curl_setblobopt(&s->blobs[BLOB_CERT_PROXY], blob);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLKEY_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL key to use for proxy\n     */\n    return Curl_setblobopt(&s->blobs[BLOB_KEY_PROXY], blob);\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CAINFO_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds CA info for SSL connection proxy.\n     * Specify entire PEM of the CA certificate\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CAINFO_BLOB))\n      return Curl_setblobopt(&s->blobs[BLOB_CAINFO_PROXY], blob);\n#endif\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_ISSUERCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds Issuer certificate to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    return Curl_setblobopt(&s->blobs[BLOB_SSL_ISSUERCERT_PROXY], blob);\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSLKEY_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL key to use\n     */\n    return Curl_setblobopt(&s->blobs[BLOB_KEY], blob);\n  case CURLOPT_CAINFO_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds CA info for SSL connection.\n     * Specify entire PEM of the CA certificate\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CAINFO_BLOB)) {\n      s->ssl.custom_cablob = TRUE;\n      return Curl_setblobopt(&s->blobs[BLOB_CAINFO], blob);\n    }\n#endif\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n  case CURLOPT_ISSUERCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds Issuer certificate to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_ISSUERCERT_BLOB))\n      return Curl_setblobopt(&s->blobs[BLOB_SSL_ISSUERCERT], blob);\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n\n  default:\n    return CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n  }\n  /* unreachable */\n}\n\n/*\n * Do not make Curl_vsetopt() static: it is called from\n * projects/OS400/ccsidcurl.c.\n */\nCURLcode Curl_vsetopt(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option, va_list param)\n{\n  if(option < CURLOPTTYPE_OBJECTPOINT)\n    return setopt_long(data, option, va_arg(param, long));\n  else if(option < CURLOPTTYPE_FUNCTIONPOINT) {\n    /* unfortunately, different pointer types cannot be identified any other\n       way than being listed explicitly */\n    switch(option) {\n    case CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER:\n    case CURLOPT_QUOTE:\n    case CURLOPT_POSTQUOTE:\n    case CURLOPT_TELNETOPTIONS:\n    case CURLOPT_PREQUOTE:\n    case CURLOPT_HTTP200ALIASES:\n    case CURLOPT_MAIL_RCPT:\n    case CURLOPT_RESOLVE:\n    case CURLOPT_PROXYHEADER:\n    case CURLOPT_CONNECT_TO:\n      return setopt_slist(data, option, va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *));\n    case CURLOPT_HTTPPOST:         /* curl_httppost * */\n    case CURLOPT_MIMEPOST:         /* curl_mime * */\n    case CURLOPT_STDERR:           /* FILE * */\n    case CURLOPT_SHARE:            /* CURLSH * */\n    case CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS:   /* CURL * */\n    case CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E: /* CURL * */\n      return setopt_pointers(data, option, param);\n    default:\n      break;\n    }\n    /* the char pointer options */\n    return setopt_cptr(data, option, va_arg(param, char *));\n  }\n  else if(option < CURLOPTTYPE_OFF_T)\n    return setopt_func(data, option, param);\n  else if(option < CURLOPTTYPE_BLOB)\n    return setopt_offt(data, option, va_arg(param, curl_off_t));\n  return setopt_blob(data, option, va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n}\n\n/*\n * curl_easy_setopt() is the external interface for setting options on an\n * easy handle.\n *\n * NOTE: This is one of few API functions that are allowed to be called from\n * within a callback.\n */\n\n#undef curl_easy_setopt\nCURLcode curl_easy_setopt(CURL *d, CURLoption tag, ...)\n{\n  va_list arg;\n  CURLcode result;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = d;\n\n  if(!data)\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n  va_start(arg, tag);\n\n  result = Curl_vsetopt(data, tag, arg);\n\n  va_end(arg);\n  if(result == CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT)\n    failf(data, \"setopt 0x%x got bad argument\", tag);\n  return result;\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":2303,"oldLines":6,"newStart":2303,"newLines":53,"lines":["     if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS))","       return Curl_setstropt(&s->str[STRING_SSL_SIGNATURE_ALGORITHMS], ptr);","     return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;","+  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY:","+    /*","+     * Set HTTP/3 stream priority (RFC 9218)","+     * Format: \"u=N[,i]\" where N is urgency (0-7) and i is incremental flag","+     */","+#ifdef USE_HTTP3","+    if(ptr) {","+      const char *p = ptr;","+      unsigned long urgency = 0;","+      bool incremental = FALSE;","+","+      /* Parse urgency: u=N */","+      if(p[0] == 'u' && p[1] == '=') {","+        char *endp = NULL;","+        p += 2;","+        urgency = strtoul(p, &endp, 10);","+        if(urgency > 7)","+          return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;","+        p = endp;","+","+        /* Check for incremental flag: ,i */","+        if(p && *p == ',') {","+          p++;","+          if(*p == 'i') {","+            incremental = TRUE;","+            p++;","+          }","+        }","+        /* Ensure string ends properly */","+        if(p && *p != '\\0')","+          return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;","+","+        s->h3_stream_priority_urgency = (uint8_t)urgency;","+        s->h3_stream_priority_incremental = incremental;","+      }","+      else","+        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;","+    }","+    else {","+      /* Reset to defaults */","+      s->h3_stream_priority_urgency = 3;","+      s->h3_stream_priority_incremental = FALSE;","+    }","+    break;","+#else","+    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;","+#endif","   case CURLOPT_PINNEDPUBLICKEY:","     /*","      * Set pinned public key for SSL connection."]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

    ARG_BOOL|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_SSL_ALLOW_BEAST},\n  {\"ssl-auto-client-cert\",       ARG_BOOL|ARG_TLS, ' ',\n   C_SSL_AUTO_CLIENT_CERT},\n  {\"ssl-no-revoke\",              ARG_BOOL|ARG_TLS, ' ', ARG_STRG, 't', C_TELNET_OPTION},\n#ifdef DEBUGBUILD\n  {\"test-duphandle\",             ARG_BOOL, ' ', C_TEST_DUPHANDLE},\n  {\"test-event\",                 ARG_BOOL, ' ', C_TEST_EVENT},\n#endif\n  {\"tftp-blksize\",               ARG_STRG, ' ', C_TFTP_BLKSIZE},\n  {\"tftp-no-options\",            ARG_BOOL, ' ', C_TFTP_NO_OPTIONS},\n  {\"time-cond\",                  ARG_STRG, 'z', C_TIME_COND},\n  {\"tls-earlydata\",              ARG_BOOL|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_TLS_EARLYDATA},\n  {\"tls-max\",                    ARG_STRG|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_TLS_MAX},\n  {\"tls13-ciphers\",              ARG_STRG|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_TLS13_CIPHERS},\n  {\"tlsauthtype\",                ARG_STRG|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_TLSAUTHTYPE},\n  {\"tlspassword\",              ARG_STRG|ARG_TLS|ARG_CLEAR, ' ', C_TLSPASSWORD},\n  {\"tlsuser\",                    ARG_STRG|ARG_TLS|ARG_CLEAR, ' ', C_TLSUSER},\n  {\"tlsv1\",                      ARG_NONE|ARG_TLS, '1', C_TLSV1},\n  {\"tlsv1.0\",                    ARG_NONE|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_TLSV1_0},\n  {\"tlsv1.1\",                    ARG_NONE|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_TLSV1_1},\n  {\"tlsv1.2\",                    ARG_NONE|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_TLSV1_2},\n  {\"tlsv1.3\",                    ARG_NONE|ARG_TLS, ' ', C_TLSV1_3},\n  {\"tr-encoding\",                ARG_BOOL, ' ', C_TR_ENCODING},\n  {\"trace\",                      ARG_FILE, ' ', C_TRACE},\n  {\"trace-ascii\",                ARG_FILE, ' ', C_TRACE_ASCII},\n  {\"trace-config\",               ARG_STRG, ' ', C_TRACE_CONFIG},\n  {\"trace-ids\",                  ARG_BOOL, ' ', C_TRACE_IDS},\n  {\"trace-time\",                 ARG_BOOL, ' ', C_TRACE_TIME},\n  {\"unix-socket\",                ARG_FILE, ' ', C_UNIX_SOCKET},\n  {\"upload-file\",                ARG_FILE, 'T', C_UPLOAD_FILE},\n  {\"upload-flags\",               ARG_STRG, ' ', C_UPLOAD_FLAGS},\n  {\"url\",                        ARG_STRG, ' ', C_URL},\n  {\"url-query\",                  ARG_STRG, ' ', C_URL_QUERY},\n  {\"use-ascii\",                  ARG_BOOL, 'B', C_USE_ASCII},\n  {\"user\",                       ARG_STRG|ARG_CLEAR, 'u', C_USER},\n  {\"user-agent\",                 ARG_STRG, 'A', C_USER_AGENT},\n  {\"variable\",                   ARG_STRG, ' ', C_VARIABLE},\n  {\"verbose\",                    ARG_BOOL, 'v', C_VERBOSE},\n  {\"version\",                    ARG_BOOL, 'V', C_VERSION},\n  {\"vlan-priority\",              ARG_STRG, ' ', C_VLAN_PRIORITY},\n#ifdef USE_WATT32\n  {\"wdebug\",                     ARG_BOOL, ' ', C_WDEBUG},\n#endif\n  {\"write-out\",                  ARG_STRG, 'w', C_WRITE_OUT},\n  {\"xattr\",                      ARG_BOOL, ' ', C_XATTR},\n};\n\n/* Split the argument of -E to 'certname' and 'passphrase' separated by colon.\n * We allow ':' and '\\' to be escaped by '\\' so that we can use certificate\n * nicknames containing ':'. See <https://sourceforge.net/p/curl/bugs/1196/>\n * for details.\n *\n * Unit test 1394\n */\nUNITTEST ParameterError parse_cert_parameter(const char *cert_parameter,\n                                             char **certname,\n                                             char **passphrase)\n{\n  size_t param_length = strlen(cert_parameter);\n  size_t span;\n  const char *param_place = NULL;\n  char *certname_place = NULL;\n  ParameterError err = PARAM_OK;\n  *certname = NULL;\n  *passphrase = NULL;\n\n  /* most trivial assumption: cert_parameter is empty */\n  if(param_length == 0)\n    return PARAM_BLANK_STRING;\n\n  /* next less trivial: cert_parameter starts 'pkcs11:' and thus\n   * looks like a RFC7512 PKCS#11 URI which can be used as-is.\n   * Also if cert_parameter contains no colon nor backslash, this\n   * means no passphrase was given and no characters escaped */\n  if(curl_strnequal(cert_parameter, \"pkcs11:\", 7) ||\n     !strpbrk(cert_parameter, \":\\\\\")) {\n    *certname = curlx_strdup(cert_parameter);\n    if(!*certname)\n      return PARAM_NO_MEM;\n    return PARAM_OK;\n  }\n  /* deal with escaped chars; find unescaped colon if it exists */\n  certname_place = curlx_malloc(param_length + 1);\n  if(!certname_place) {\n    err = PARAM_NO_MEM;\n    goto done;\n  }\n\n  *certname = certname_place;\n  param_place = cert_parameter;\n  while(*param_place) {\n    span = strcspn(param_place, \":\\\\\");\n    memcpy(certname_place, param_place, span);\n    param_place += span;\n    certname_place += span;\n    /* we just ate all the non-special chars. now we are on either a special\n     * char or the end of the string. */\n    switch(*param_place) {\n    case '\\0':\n      break;\n    case '\\\\':\n      param_place++;\n      switch(*param_place) {\n      case '\\0':\n        *certname_place++ = '\\\\';\n        break;\n      case '\\\\':\n        *certname_place++ = '\\\\';\n        param_place++;\n        break;\n      case ':':\n        *certname_place++ = ':';\n        param_place++;\n        break;\n      default:\n        *certname_place++ = '\\\\';\n        *certname_place++ = *param_place;\n        param_place++;\n        break;\n      }\n      break;\n    case ':':\n      /* Since we live in a world of weirdness and confusion, the Windows\n         dudes can use : when using drive letters and thus c:\\file:password\n         needs to work. In order not to break compatibility, we still use : as\n         separator, but we try to detect when it is used for a filename! On\n         Windows. */\n#ifdef _WIN32\n      if((param_place == &cert_parameter[1]) &&\n         (cert_parameter[2] == '\\\\' || cert_parameter[2] == '/') &&\n         ISALPHA(cert_parameter[0])) {\n        /* colon in the second column, followed by a backslash, and the\n           first character is an alphabetic letter:\n\n           this is a drive letter colon */\n        *certname_place++ = ':';\n        param_place++;\n        break;\n      }\n#endif\n      /* escaped colons and Windows drive letter colons were handled\n       * above; if we are still here, this is a separating colon */\n      param_place++;\n      if(*param_place) {\n        *passphrase = curlx_strdup(param_place);\n        if(!*passphrase)\n          err = PARAM_NO_MEM;\n      }\n      goto done;\n    }\n  }\ndone:\n  if(err) {\n    tool_safefree(*certname);\n  }\n  else\n    *certname_place = '\\0';\n  return err;\n}\n\n/* Replace (in-place) '%20' by '+' according to RFC1866 */\nstatic size_t replace_url_encoded_space_by_plus(char *url)\n{\n  size_t orig_len = strlen(url);\n  size_t orig_index = 0;\n  size_t new_index = 0;\n\n  while(orig_index < orig_len) {\n    if((url[orig_index] == '%') &&\n       (url[orig_index + 1] == '2') &&\n       (url[orig_index + 2] == '0')) {\n      url[new_index] = '+';\n      orig_index += 3;\n    }\n    else {\n      if(new_index != orig_index) {\n        url[new_index] = url[orig_index];\n      }\n      orig_index++;\n    }\n    new_index++;\n  }\n\n  url[new_index] = 0; /* terminate string */\n\n  return new_index; /* new size */\n}\n\nstatic ParameterError GetFileAndPassword(const char *nextarg, char **file,\n                                         char **password)\n{\n  char *certname, *passphrase;\n  ParameterError err;\n  /* nextarg is never NULL here */\n  err = parse_cert_parameter(nextarg, &certname, &passphrase);\n  if(!err) {\n    curlx_free(*file);\n    *file = certname;\n    if(passphrase) {\n      curlx_free(*password);\n      *password = passphrase;\n    }\n  }\n  return err;\n}\n\nstruct sizeunit {\n  char unit; /* single lowercase ASCII letter */\n  curl_off_t mul;\n  size_t mlen; /* number of digits in 'mul', when written in decimal */\n};\n\nstatic const struct sizeunit *getunit(char unit)\n{\n  static const struct sizeunit list[] = {\n    {'p', (curl_off_t)1125899906842624, 16 }, /* Peta */\n    {'t', (curl_off_t)1099511627776,    13 }, /* Tera */\n    {'g', 1073741824,                   10 }, /* Giga */\n    {'m', 1048576,                       7 }, /* Mega */\n    {'k', 1024,                          4 }, /* Kilo */\n  };\n\n  size_t i;\n  for(i = 0; i < CURL_ARRAYSIZE(list); i++)\n    if((unit | 0x20) == list[i].unit)\n      return &list[i];\n  return NULL;\n}\n\n/* Get a size parameter for '--limit-rate' or '--max-filesize'.\n   We support P, T, G, M and K (case insensitive) suffixes.\n\n   Unit test 1623\n */\nUNITTEST ParameterError GetSizeParameter(const char *arg, curl_off_t *out)\n{\n  const char *unit = arg;\n  curl_off_t value;\n  curl_off_t prec = 0;\n  size_t plen = 0;\n  curl_off_t add = 0;\n  curl_off_t mul = 1;\n  int rc;\n\n  rc = curlx_str_number(&unit, &value, CURL_OFF_T_MAX);\n  if(rc == STRE_OVERFLOW)\n    return PARAM_NUMBER_TOO_LARGE;\n  else if(rc)\n    return PARAM_BAD_NUMERIC;\n\n  if(!curlx_str_single(&unit, '.')) {\n    const char *s = unit;\n    if(curlx_str_number(&unit, &prec, CURL_OFF_T_MAX))\n      return PARAM_BAD_NUMERIC;\n    plen = unit - s;\n  }\n\n  if(strlen(unit) > 1)\n    return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n  else if(!*unit || ((*unit | 0x20) == 'b')) {\n    if(plen)\n      /* cannot handle partial bytes */\n      return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n  }\n  else {\n    const struct sizeunit *su = getunit(*unit);\n    if(!su)\n      return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n    mul = su->mul;\n\n    if(prec) {\n      /* precision was provided */\n      curl_off_t frac = 1;\n\n      /* too many precision digits, trim them */\n      while(su->mlen <= plen) {\n        prec /= 10;\n        plen--;\n      }\n\n      while(plen--)\n        frac *= 10;\n\n      if((CURL_OFF_T_MAX / mul) > prec)\n        add = mul * prec / frac;\n      else\n        add = (mul / frac) * prec;\n    }\n  }\n  if(value > ((CURL_OFF_T_MAX - add) / mul))\n    return PARAM_NUMBER_TOO_LARGE;\n\n  *out = (value * mul) + add;\n  return PARAM_OK;\n}\n\n#ifdef HAVE_WRITABLE_ARGV\nstatic void cleanarg(char *str)\n{\n  /* now that getstr has copied the contents of nextarg, wipe the next\n   * argument out so that the username:password is not displayed in the\n   * system process list */\n  if(str) {\n    size_t len = strlen(str);\n    memset(str, '*', len);\n  }\n}\n#else\n#define cleanarg(x) tool_nop_stmt\n#endif\n\n/* the maximum size we allow the dynbuf generated string */\n#define MAX_DATAURLENCODE (500 * 1024 * 1024)\n\n/* --data-urlencode */\nstatic ParameterError data_urlencode(const char *nextarg,\n                                     char **postp,\n                                     size_t *lenp)\n{\n  /* [name]=[content], we encode the content part only\n   * [name]@[filename]\n   *\n   * Case 2: we first load the file using that name and then encode\n   * the content.\n   */\n  ParameterError err;\n  const char *p = strchr(nextarg, '=');\n  size_t nlen;\n  char is_file;\n  char *postdata = NULL;\n  size_t size = 0;\n  if(!p)\n    /* there was no '=' letter, check for a '@' instead */\n    p = strchr(nextarg, '@');\n  if(p) {\n    nlen = p - nextarg; /* length of the name part */\n    is_file = *p++; /* pass the separator */\n  }\n  else {\n    /* neither @ nor =, so no name and it is not a file */\n    nlen = 0;\n    is_file = 0;\n    p = nextarg;\n  }\n  if('@' == is_file) {\n    FILE *file;\n    /* a '@' letter, it means that a filename or - (stdin) follows */\n    if(!strcmp(\"-\", p)) {\n      file = stdin;\n      CURL_BINMODE(stdin);\n    }\n    else {\n      file = curlx_fopen(p, \"rb\");\n      if(!file) {\n        errorf(\"Failed to open %s\", p);\n        return PARAM_READ_ERROR;\n      }\n    }\n\n    err = file2memory(&postdata, &size, file);\n\n    if(file && (file != stdin))\n      curlx_fclose(file);\n    if(err)\n      return err;\n  }\n  else {\n    err = getstr(&postdata, p, ALLOW_BLANK);\n    if(err)\n      goto error;\n    size = strlen(postdata);\n  }\n\n  if(!postdata) {\n    /* no data from the file, point to a zero byte string to make this\n       get sent as a POST anyway */\n    postdata = curlx_strdup(\"\");\n    if(!postdata)\n      return PARAM_NO_MEM;\n    size = 0;\n  }\n  else {\n    char *enc = curl_easy_escape(NULL, postdata, (int)size);\n    tool_safefree(postdata); /* no matter if it worked or not */\n    if(enc) {\n      char *n;\n      replace_url_encoded_space_by_plus(enc);\n      if(nlen > 0) { /* only append '=' if we have a name */\n        struct dynbuf dyn;\n        curlx_dyn_init(&dyn, MAX_DATAURLENCODE);\n        if(curlx_dyn_addn(&dyn, nextarg, nlen) ||\n           curlx_dyn_addn(&dyn, \"=\", 1) ||\n           curlx_dyn_add(&dyn, enc)) {\n          curl_free(enc);\n          return PARAM_NO_MEM;\n        }\n        curl_free(enc);\n        n = curlx_dyn_ptr(&dyn);\n        size = curlx_dyn_len(&dyn);\n      }\n      else {\n        n = enc;\n        size = strlen(n);\n      }\n      postdata = n;\n    }\n    else\n      return PARAM_NO_MEM;\n  }\n  *postp = postdata;\n  *lenp = size;\n  return PARAM_OK;\nerror:\n  return err;\n}\n\nstatic void sethttpver(struct OperationConfig *config, long httpversion)\n{\n  if(config->httpversion &&\n     (config->httpversion != httpversion))\n    warnf(\"Overrides previous HTTP version option\");\n\n  config->httpversion = httpversion;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode set_trace_config(const char *token)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  const char *next, *name;\n  bool toggle;\n\n  while(token) {\n    size_t len;\n    next = strchr(token, ',');\n\n    if(next)\n      len = next - token;\n    else\n      len = strlen(token);\n\n    switch(*token) {\n    case '-':\n      toggle = FALSE;\n      name = token + 1;\n      len--;\n      break;\n    case '+':\n      toggle = TRUE;\n      name = token + 1;\n      len--;\n      break;\n    default:\n      toggle = TRUE;\n      name = token;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    if((len == 3) && curl_strnequal(name, \"all\", 3)) {\n      global->traceids = toggle;\n      global->tracetime = toggle;\n      if(toggle)\n        result = curl_global_trace(\"all,-lib-ids\");\n      else\n        result = curl_global_trace(token);\n      if(result)\n        goto out;\n    }\n    else if((len == 3) && curl_strnequal(name, \"ids\", 3)) {\n      global->traceids = toggle;\n    }\n    else if((len == 4) && curl_strnequal(name, \"time\", 4)) {\n      global->tracetime = toggle;\n    }\n    else {\n      char buffer[64];\n      curl_msnprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), \"%c%.*s,-lib-ids\",\n                     toggle ? '+' : '-', (int)len, name);\n      result = curl_global_trace(buffer);\n      if(result)\n        goto out;\n    }\n    if(next) {\n      next++;\n      if(*next == ' ')\n        next++;\n    }\n    token = next;\n  }\nout:\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic int findarg(const void *a, const void *b)\n{\n  const struct LongShort *aa = a;\n  const struct LongShort *bb = b;\n  return strcmp(aa->lname, bb->lname);\n}\n\nconst struct LongShort *findshortopt(char letter)\n{\n  static const struct LongShort *singles[128 - ' ']; /* ASCII => pointer */\n  static bool singles_done = FALSE;\n  if((letter >= 127) || (letter <= ' '))\n    return NULL;\n\n  if(!singles_done) {\n    unsigned int j;\n    for(j = 0; j < CURL_ARRAYSIZE(aliases); j++) {\n      if(aliases[j].letter != ' ') {\n        unsigned char l = (unsigned char)aliases[j].letter;\n        singles[l - ' '] = &aliases[j];\n      }\n    }\n    singles_done = TRUE;\n  }\n  return singles[letter - ' '];\n}\n\nstruct TOSEntry {\n  const strcmp(aa->name, bb->name);\n}\n\n#define MAX_QUERY_LEN 100000 /* larger is not likely to ever work */\nstatic ParameterError url_query(const char *nextarg,\n                                struct OperationConfig *config)\n{\n  size_t size = 0;\n  ParameterError err = PARAM_OK;\n  char *query;\n  struct dynbuf dyn;\n  curlx_dyn_init(&dyn, MAX_QUERY_LEN);\n\n  if(*nextarg == '+') {\n    /* use cmd,\n                               const char *nextarg,\n                               struct OperationConfig *config)\n{\n  char *postdata = NULL;\n  FILE *file;\n  size_t size = 0;\n  ParameterError err = PARAM_OK;\n\n  if(cmd == C_DATA_URLENCODE) { /* --data-urlencode */\n    err = data_urlencode(nextarg, &postdata, &size);\n    if(err)\n      return err;\n  }\n  else if('@' == *nextarg && (cmd != C_DATA_RAW)) {\n    /* the data begins with a '@' letter, it means that a filename\n       or - (stdin) follows */\n    nextarg++; /* pass the @ */\n\n    if(!strcmp(\"-\", nextarg)) {\n      file = stdin;\n      if(cmd == C_DATA_BINARY) /* forced data-binary */\n        CURL_BINMODE(stdin);\n    }\n    else {\n      file = curlx_fopen(nextarg, \"rb\");\n      if(!file) {\n        errorf(\"Failed to open %s\", nextarg);\n        return PARAM_READ_ERROR;\n      }\n    }\n\n    if((cmd == C_DATA_BINARY) || /* --data-binary */\n       (cmd == C_JSON) /* --json */)\n PARAM_NO_MEM;\n\n  tool_safefree(postdata);\n\n  config->postfields = curlx_dyn_ptr(&config->postdata);\n  return err;\n}\n\nstatic ParameterError set_rate(const char *nextarg)\n{\n  /* --rate */\n  /* support a few different suffixes, extract the suffix first, then\n     get the number and convert to per hour.\n     /s == per second\n     /m == per minute\n     /h == per hour (default)\n     /d == per day (24 hours)\n   */\n  ParameterError err = PARAM_OK;\n  const char *p = nextarg;\n  curl_off_t denominator;\n  curl_off_t numerator = 60 * 60 * 1000; /* default per hour */\n\n  if(curlx_str_number(&p, &denominator, CURL_OFF_T_MAX))\n    return PARAM_BAD_NUMERIC;\n\n  if(denominator < 1)\n    return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n\n  if(!curlx_str_single(&p, '/')) {\n    curl_off_t numunits;\n\n    if(curlx_str_number(&p, &numunits, CURL_OFF_T_MAX))\n      numunits = 1;\n\n    switch(*p) {\n    case 's': /* per second */\n      numerator = 1000;\n      break;\n    case 'm': /* per minute */\n      numerator = 60 * 1000;\n      break;\n    case 'h': /* per hour */\n      break;\n    case 'd': /* per day */\n      numerator = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n      break;\n    default:\n      errorf(\"unsupported --rate unit\");\n      err = PARAM_BAD_USE;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    if((CURL_OFF_T_MAX / numerator) < numunits) {\n      /* overflow, too large number */\n      errorf(\"too large --rate unit\");\n      err = PARAM_NUMBER_TOO_LARGE;\n    }\n    else\n      /* this typecast is okay based on the check above */\n      numerator *= numunits;\n  }\n\n  if(err)\n    ;\n  else if(denominator > numerator)\n    err = PARAM_NUMBER_TOO_LARGE;\n  else\n    global->ms_per_transfer = numerator / denominator;\n\n  return err;\n}\n\nconst struct LongShort *findlongopt(const char *opt)\n{\n  struct LongShort key;\n  key.lname = opt;\n\n  return bsearch(&key, aliases, CURL_ARRAYSIZE(aliases),\n                 sizeof(aliases[0]), findarg);\n}\n\nstatic ParameterError add_url(struct OperationConfig *config,\n                              const char *thisurl,\n                              bool remote_noglob)\n{\n  ParameterError err = PARAM_OK;\n  struct getout *url;\n\n  if(!config->url_get)\n    config->url_get = config->url_list;\n\n  if(config->url_get) {\n    /* there is a node here, if it already is filled-in continue to find\n       an \"empty\" node */\n    while(config->url_get && config->url_get->urlset)\n      config->url_get = config->url_get->next;\n  }\n\n  /* now there might or might not be an available node to fill in! */\n\n  if(config->url_get)\n    /* existing node */\n    url = config->url_get;\n  else\n    /* there was no free node, create one! */\n    config->url_get = url = new_getout(config);\n\n  if(!url)\n    return PARAM_NO_MEM;\n  else {\n    /* fill in the URL */\n    err = getstr(&url->url, thisurl, DENY_BLANK);\n    url->urlset = TRUE;\n    if(remote_noglob)\n      url->useremote = url->noglob = TRUE;\n    if(!err && (++config->num_urls > 1) &&\n       (config->etag_save_file || config->etag_compare_file)) {\n      errorf(\"The etag options only work on a single URL\");\n      return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n    }\n  }\n  return err;\n}\n\nstatic ParameterError parse_url(struct OperationConfig *config,\n                                const char *nextarg)\n{\n  /* nextarg is never NULL here */\n  if(nextarg[0] == '@') {\n    /* read URLs from a file, treat all as -O */\n    struct dynbuf line;\n    ParameterError err = PARAM_OK;\n    bool error = FALSE;\n    bool fromstdin = !strcmp(\"-\", &nextarg[1]);\n    FILE *f;\n\n    if(fromstdin)\n      f = stdin;\n    else\n      f = curlx_fopen(&nextarg[1], FOPEN_READTEXT);\n    if(f) {\n      curlx_dyn_init(&line, 8092);\n      while(my_get_line(f, &line, &error)) {\n        const char *ptr = curlx_dyn_ptr(&line);\n        err = add_url(config, ptr, TRUE);\n        if(err)\n          break;\n      }\n      if(!fromstdin)\n        curlx_fclose(f);\n      curlx_dyn_free(&line);\n      if(error || err)\n        return PARAM_READ_ERROR;\n      return PARAM_OK;\n    }\n    return PARAM_READ_ERROR; /* file not found */\n  }\n  return add_url(config, nextarg, FALSE);\n}\n\nstatic ParameterError parse_localport(struct OperationConfig *config,\n                                      const char *nextarg)\n{\n  const char *pp = NULL;\n  const char *p = nextarg;\n  char buffer[22];\n  size_t plen = 0;\n  while(ISDIGIT(*p))\n    p++;\n  plen = p - nextarg;\n  if(*p) {\n    pp = p;\n    /* check for ' - [end]' */\n    if(ISBLANK(*pp))\n      pp++;\n    if(*pp != '-')\n      return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n    pp++;\n    if(ISBLANK(*pp))\n      pp++;\n  }\n  curl_msnprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), \"%.*s\", (int)plen, nextarg);\n  if(str2unummax(&config->localport, buffer, 65535))\n    return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n  if(!pp)\n    config->localportrange = 1; /* default number of ports to try */\n  else {\n    if(str2unummax(&config->localportrange, pp, 65535))\n      return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n    config->localportrange -= (config->localport - 1);\n    if(config->localportrange < 1)\n      return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n  }\n  return PARAM_OK;\n}\n\nstatic ParameterError parse_continue_at(struct OperationConfig *config,\n                                        const char *nextarg)\n{\n  ParameterError --no-clobber\");\n    return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n  }\n  /* This makes us continue an ftp transfer at given position */\n  if(strcmp(nextarg, \"-\")) {\n    err = str2offset(&config->resume_from, nextarg);\n    config->resume_from_current = FALSE;\n  }\n  else {\n    config->resume_from_current = TRUE;\n    config->resume_from = 0;\n  }\n  config->use_resume = TRUE;\n  return err;\n}\n\nstatic ParameterError parse_ech(struct OperationConfig *config,\n                                const char *nextarg)\n{\n  ParameterError err = PARAM_OK;\n  if(!feature_ech)\n    err = ParameterError parse_header(struct OperationConfig *config,\n                                   cmdline_t cmd,\n                                   const char *nextarg)\n{\n  ParameterError err = PARAM_OK;\n\n  /* A custom header to append to a list */\n  if(nextarg[0] == '@') {\n    /* read many headers from a file or stdin */\n    bool use_stdin = !strcmp(&nextarg[1], \"-\");\n    FILE *file = use_stdin ? stdin : curlx_fopen(&nextarg[1], FOPEN_READTEXT);\n    if(!file) {\n      errorf(\"Failed to open %s\", &nextarg[1]);\n      err = PARAM_READ_ERROR;\n    }\n    else {\n      struct ';')) {\n      warnf(\"The provided %s header '%s' does not look like a header?\",\n            (cmd == C_PROXY_HEADER) ? \"proxy\" : \"HTTP\", nextarg);\n    }\n    if(cmd == C_PROXY_HEADER) /* --proxy-header */\n      err = add2list(&config->proxyheaders, nextarg);\n    else\n      err = add2list(&config->headers, nextarg);\n  }\n  return err;\n}\n\nstatic ParameterError parse_output(struct OperationConfig *config,\n                                   const char *nextarg)\n{\n  ParameterError err = PARAM_OK;\n  struct getout *url;\n\n  /* output file */\n  if(!config->url_out)\n    config->url_out = config->url_list;\n  if(config->url_out) {\n    /* there is a node here, if it already is filled-in continue to find\n       an \"empty\" node */\n    while(config->url_out && config->url_out->outset)\n      config->url_out = config->url_out->next;\n  }\n\n  /* now there might or might not be an available node to fill in! */\n\n  if(config->url_out)\n    /* existing node
